title: Leadership in Honors
description: Students will develop their leadership skills and identity as a leader, while completing a leadership project on campus or in the community. Students reflect on their experiential learning about leadership through dialog and ongoing reflective practice.

title: Fellowships and Awards
description: Students prepare and apply to a prestigious national or international fellowship, scholarship, or grant. Provides students with interview preparation, materials review, and general instruction in the common genres of advanced award applications.

title: Honors Advocacy and Engagement
description: Practicum: 1This course allows students to engage in a critical leadership role by serving as an honors college advocate. Students will explore leadership concepts, communication with different audiences, and collaborative problem-solving. They will put these skills into practice by supporting college initiatives in a variety of ways, including peer advising, recruitment efforts, event planning, and alumni engagement.

title: Honors Advocacy and Engagement II
description: Practicum: 1This is the second part of a two-course experience. In Part 1, students learned about institutional communication and collaborative problem-solving and put those skills into practice through advocacy and engagement work for the Honors College. In Part 2, students will take on advocacy roles with greater responsibility and independence, emphasizing collaboration with other university units and public-facing communication.

title: Honors Study Abroad Experience-Seniors
description: Honors study abroad experience for seniors.

title: Honors Senior Thesis or Project
description: Research and writing/preparation of Honors Senior Thesis or Honors Senior Project.

title: Honors Directed Study-Seniors
description: Study of a problem or topic in collaboration with a faculty member in the discipline.

title: Honors Internship Experience-Seniors
description: Honors internship experience for seniors.

title: Honors Independent Study-Seniors
description: Individual research, scholarly, or creative projects under the supervision of a faculty member.

title: Information Systems in Business
description: A study of information systems in business to include management decision support systems, concepts of systems analysis and design, and information processing. Students will learn to use and apply various software programs as productivity tools.

title: Fundamentals of Business Programming
description: Fundamental skills and knowledge for programming business applications including interface design, writing documentation, and using debugging tools. Procedural programming structures and design considerations and interface object use are presented in a state-of-the-art integrated development environment.

title: Introduction to Business Analytics
description: An introduction to structured data and the relational model. An introduction to querying of structured data, it's extraction and simple analyses in a spreadsheet environment.

title: Business Analytics Programming
description: Covers fundamentals of programming needed to write scripts to extract, analyze and present data in a business intelligence setting. No previous programming experience is necessary. Provides a foundation for all business analytics courses in the College of Business.

title: Business Process Modeling
description: Introduces concept of business processes. Major business processes are described along with their interactions with each other. Flow diagrams and other representations of processes are introduced. Process innovation, improvement and documentation are addressed.

title: Advanced Business Programming
description: Develops object-oriented programming skills at the intermediate level. Develops techniques of data access, input, output, and processing in a business environment using programming tools. Utilizes beginning SQL programming techniques and a state-of-the-art integrated development environment.

title: System Development Methodologies
description: This course covers systems development methodologies in use by the Information Systems profession. Sequencing, activities and process characteristics are examined for each methodology. Appropriate tools to support each methodology are introduced and used. A decision framework for the selection of an appropriate methodology will be used.

title: Database Management Systems
description: Study of physical and logical design and structure of relational databases; Advanced SQL; hands-on experience with enterprise database management software. Introduction to storage and manipulation of semi-structured and unstructured data.

title: Computer Networks
description: Study of enterprise networking infrastructure. Theory and practice of topics includes network models, network hardware and software, and network security.

title: AI Systems in Business
description: This course explores the strategic and technical applications of artificial intelligence in business, with a focus on systems-level integration and responsible use. Students examine how AI tools—including generative AI—can enhance communication, analytics, marketing, operations, and innovation within enterprise environments. Through hands-on exercises, case studies, and applied projects, students will learn to design, test, and evaluate AI-driven business solutions using structured workflows, multi-platform tools, and data-informed decision-making. The course emphasizes ethical and risk-aware implementation, grounded in the technical competencies of the information systems field.

title: Information Assurance
description: Conceptual frameworks for information assurance: asset valuation, security threats, risk assessment, policy development, policy implementation, controls, business continuity planning, and disaster recovery. Communication and analytical skills are enhanced through a case-based approach.

title: Server-based Networking & Security
description: Study of theory and practice of enterprise networking from a server operating system perspective. Topics include domain creation, Active Directory management and delegation, scripting, security policies, roaming profiles, access permissions, and security auditing.

title: Selected Topics in Information Systems
description: Selected topics in information systems. Small groups will study topics selected because of a special interest in them on the part of students or faculty.

title: Independent Study - Juniors
description: • Enrollment form available in IS department office.

title: Internship in Information Systems
description: Coordinated on-the-job business experience in information systems. Gives students an opportunity to participate actively in daily business activities related to the IS major.

title: Project Management
description: Focus on information systems projects: methodology selection, initiating, planning, executing, control and closing. Issues of time, scope, cost, quality and risk are investigated. Writing and software development skills are enhanced.

title: Seminar in Information Systems
description: In-depth study of current IS topics and discussion of current IS issues and trends. Includes integrated communication component and team-based information systems development project.

title: Advanced Business Analytics
description: The course introduces students to an array of sophisticated techniques related to business data analytics and data mining. Students will learn how to use analytical approaches to meet business needs using diverse datasets. Topics include big data, data transformation, classification, prediction, association, clustering, and performance evaluation. Practical application of these techniques in the business arena will be enabled through student experiences with appropriate software. The course is designed to provide advanced experiences in theory and practice that can be enhanced when the student moves into the workforce.

title: Selected Topics
description: Selected topics in information systems. Small groups will study topics selected because of a special interest in them on the part of students or faculty.

title: Directed Studies
description: Internship-type experiences in business situations relevant to the student's major field of study, under supervision of a faculty member.

title: Independent Study - Seniors
description: • Enrollment form available in IS department office.

title: Foundations of Integrated Studies
description: An introduction to interdisciplinary learning and inquiry through the exploration of ethical questions and sociopolitical challenges, which are considered from multiple academic viewpoints spanning the arts, natural sciences, social sciences, history, and the humanities.

title: Integrated Studies Research Seminar
description: Advanced study of research and creative methodologies used in the arts, humanities, natural sciences, and social sciences. Examines methodological similarities to enhance understanding of the common underpinnings that define liberal studies and human inquiry as well as differences in rhetorical strategies, questions asked, and tools used.

title: Directed Studies
description: For the student who wishes to pursue study of an interdisciplinary topic under the guidance of a faculty member.

title: Independent Study-Juniors
description: For the student who wishes to pursue independent study of an interdisciplinary topic under the guidance of a faculty member.

title: Integrated Studies Capstone Seminar
description: Culminating experience of the Integrated Studies Major. Focuses on critical inquiry in and complex interactions between the arts, humanities, natural sciences, and social sciences. Completion of a semester-long project related to a central course theme.

title: Internship
description: Provides applied experience in an area of interest with content related to a student's theme of study.

title: Independent Study - Seniors
description: For the student who wishes to pursue independent study of an interdisciplinary topic under the guidance of a faculty member.

title: Academic Exploration
description: Designed to assist students in their journey of determining a major area of study by connecting their interests, talents, and passions with academic offerings at UW-Eau Claire.

title: Foundations of Disability Studies
description: This course is an introductory survey to D/disability—as an identity, culture, and theoretical framework—intended for students at any level or in any discipline. We will examine definitions and models of disability, the history of the disability rights movement, intersections with other theoretical frameworks, and how disability is represented culturally and within media.

title: Brain: Introduction to Neuroscience
description: The brain and how it works including sense, movements, emotions, and the malfunctioning brain. Readings in popular publications. Emphasis on understanding how brain function is central to almost everything in day-to-day life.

title: Distance Running for Health and Fitness
description: Design and implement a distance running training plan; learn the importance of wellness as related to health, nutrition, goal setting and resilience.

title: Integrative Seminar
description: Facilitates integration between linked courses in a learning community.

title: Leadership for a Better World
description: This is an experiential course analyzing values of the Social Change Model of Leadership, exploring leadership identity/competencies, personal values, identifying strategies for positive change, and discussing current leadership issues.

title: Health Humanities
description: This course explores ideas about health through the perspectives of the humanities. Topics covered include the history of medicine and illness; bioethics; the role of narrative and other representational forms in the experience of illness, and health inequities and their causes.

title: Introduction to Narrative Medicine
description: This course introduces students to the field of Narrative Medicine, and builds a foundation in its terminology, philosophy, and application across the medical sciences and medical humanities. Students will engage with fundamental literature within the discipline, literary examples of narrative medicine, as well as texts that expand the field by introducing unique intersections that define and redefine the human body as a social, cultural, and spiritual entity in need of care throughout stages of mortal experience.

title: Experiential Internship
description: Provides career-related work that extends learning beyond the classroom. Involves intentional learning and active reflection tied to the student's academic and career interests.

title: Techniques of the Singing/Speaking Voice
description: Study of voice techniques and vocal health for the singing and speaking voice.

title: Integrating the Social Sciences: The Social Studies
description: This course intends to influence teachers' abilities to understand the nature of the social studies in relation to the integration of the social sciences and the humanities with the goal of influencing civic competence.

title: Special Topics
description: A variable content course designed to provide opportunities to address contemporary issues from multiple perspectives.

title: Integrated Performing Arts Across the Curriculum
description: An introduction to the theories and practices of dance, music, and theatre techniques appropriate for integration across curriculum grades 1-8. Open to all majors.

title: Understanding the Arts
description: This course empowers students to decode the language of the visual and performing arts. Students will consider historical and cultural contexts of works of art and develop aesthetic literacy.

title: Faculty-Led International Immersion Experience
description: This Faculty-Led International Immersion Experience (FLIIE) course is specifically utilized for any interdisciplinary FLIIE program. This hands-on experiential learning course is not suited for use in the United States or for any other international program aside from the FLIIE international Immersion experience. One, two, or three credits may be assigned to a particular offering.

title: Preparatory Course for the Central European Travel Seminar
description: Prepares students for IDIS 340 /540, the Central European Travel Seminar held during summer session. Students will study the historical and contemporary perspectives for allied disciplines.

title: Central European Travel Seminar
description: Interdisciplinary summer travel seminar occurring most often in Berlin, Prague, Brno, Vienna, Budapest, and Krakow that builds on groundwork laid in a preparatory spring semester course. Seminar involves advanced on-site study and research across three disciplines.

title: McNair Fall Seminar for Juniors
description: Seminar provides an orientation to McNair Program goals and activities, and enhances students' ability to undertake undergraduate research. Course provides an interdisciplinary perspective on the research process for students who desire to become college professors/researchers.

title: McNair Spring Seminar for Juniors
description: McNair Scholars refine the scope and design of their research projects, learn about the development of the American research community across the twentieth century, attend research presentations, and begin evaluating prospective graduate institutions and mentors.

title: International Internships
description: Virtual or in-person international internship opportunities offered by UW-Eau Claire partners at multiple locations with placement best suited to student’s academic and career goals. Internships are unpaid. To apply, contact the Center for International Education.

title: Arts Administration Seminar
description: An overview of the field of arts administration offering a practical approach examining literature, resources, programs, organizations, and individuals.

title: McNair Fall Seminar for Seniors
description: Scholars prepare and formally present their research findings to academic audiences as journal articles, conference presentations, and posters. They prepare for and take the GRE, and refine and submit application materials to graduate schools.

title: McNair Spring Seminar for Seniors
description: Seminar focuses on how universities operate and what factors drive academics' careers--tenure and academic freedom, professional ethics, diversity, faculty governance and collegiality, funding agencies/foundations and academic publishing. Also covers graduate school progress/finances.

title: Special Topics
description: A variable content course designed to provide opportunities to address contemporary issues from multiple perspectives.

title: Preparatory Course for the Central European Travel Seminar
description: Prepares students for IDIS 340 /540, the Central European Travel Seminar held during summer session. Students will study the historical and contemporary perspectives for allied disciplines.

title: Central European Travel Seminar
description: Interdisciplinary summer travel seminar occurring most often in Berlin, Prague, Brno, Vienna, Budapest, and Krakow that builds on groundwork laid in a preparatory spring semester course. Seminar involves advanced on-site study and research across three disciplines.

title: International Experience in India
description: An interdisciplinary introduction to contemporary Indian business, culture, politics, history, religion and society that includes a three-week in-country experience.

title: International Experience in China
description: An interdisciplinary introduction to contemporary Chinese business, culture, politics, history, religion and society that includes a three-week in-country experience.

title: International Experience in Thailand
description: An interdisciplinary introduction to contemporary Thailand business, culture, politics, history, religion and society that includes a three-week in-country experience. Students will learn through pre-departure and in-country curriculum the nuances of Thailand culture, business practices, communication styles and values.

title: International Business Internship Program
description: Students will participate in work experiences related to their area of interest.

title: Global Business Solutions
description: Global Business Solutions offers student consulting to international business clients facing global challenges. The students are typically seniors majoring in business who have completed all of their core business courses as well as most of the specialization courses. The students form consulting teams and work like apprentices, closely supervised by their instructor. Student consultants in this course offer Global Business Solutions in five specific areas: Global Market Entry, Cross-Cultural Management, Regulatory & Risk Analysis, Global Competitive Intelligence, and Sustainability & ESG Compliance.

title: Topics in International Business
description: Current issues in international business will be the focus of attention. Topic selection will be made by the instructor. Issues will be those facing contemporary management professionals.

title: University of Graz, Austria
description: An exchange program for one semester or the academic year. Students may take courses for international students or regular university courses. Courses offered in English and German. Students live in apartments or dormitories.

title: Ajou University, Korea
description: Students attend courses scheduled at Ajou University in South Korea. A one or two semester program, students may choose from a comprehensive curriculum and take any course for which they have appropriate prerequisites. Students live in university residence halls.

title: Kansai Gaidai University of Foreign Studies-Hirakata City, Japan
description: An exchange program for one semester or the academic year in Hirakata City near Kyoto, the old capital of Japan. Students take Asian Studies courses taught in English and Japanese language courses. Students live in dormitories or with host families.

title: University of Winchester, England
description: Fall semester exchange program with University of Winchester in Winchester, England. Students may choose from all classes for which they have appropriate prerequisites. Students live in residence halls on or near campus.

title: Ajou University, South Korea
description: Students attend courses scheduled at Ajou University's International Summer School. Courses are typically offered in business and economics, Korean language, and social sciences. Students live in university residence halls.

title: Murdoch University-Perth, Australia
description: Students can attend courses at Murdoch University during either semester or for the calendar year. Students may choose from a comprehensive curriculum, and take any courses for which they have met the appropriate prerequisites. Housing is in the university apartments.

title: University of Limerick, Ireland
description: A one or two semester exchange program with the University of Limerick in Limerick, Ireland. Students may choose from all classes for which they have appropriate prerequisites. Students live in university residence halls.

title: Southern Cross University-Lismore, Australia
description: An exchange program for one semester or the calendar year with Southern Cross University. Students may choose from all classes for which they have appropriate prerequisites. Students live in university apartments.

title: Southern Cross University-Gold Coast, Australia
description: An exchange program for one semester or the calendar year with Southern Cross University. Students may choose from all classes for which they have appropriate prerequisites. Students live in university apartments.

title: Linnaeus University, Sweden
description: An exchange program for one semester or academic year with Linnaeus University. Linnaeus has a wide course selection but is primarily known for its courses in the business, economic, social, and teaching sectors. International courses are taught in English. Students live in university accommodation.

title: FHS-Fulda
description: A one semester or academic year program through the Hessen-Wisconsin exchange. Students may choose from all classes for which they have appropriate prerequisites and live in university residence halls.

title: University of Aberdeen, Aberdeen, Scotland
description: A one or two semester exchange program with the University of Aberdeen in Aberdeen, Scotland. Students may choose from all classes for which they have appropriate prerequisites. Students live in university residence halls.

title: Universität-Marburg
description: A one semester or academic year exchange. Prior German language study required for regular university classes. International Undergraduate Studies Program is open to students with no prior study of German. Housing is in university residences.

title: University of Glasgow, Glasgow, Scotland
description: A one or two semester exchange program with the University of Glasgow in Glasgow, Scotland. Students may choose from all classes for which they have appropriate prerequisites. Students live in university residence halls.

title: University of Stirling, Stirling, Scotland
description: A one or two semester exchange program with the University of Stirling in Stirling, Scotland. Students may choose from all classes for which they have appropriate prerequisites. Students live in university residence halls.

title: Chile, Pontificia Universidad Católica de Valparaíso
description: Students attending the Pontificia Universidad Católica de Valparaíso take Spanish language courses, along with regular university offerings. Students live with host families.

title: University of Stellenbosch, South Africa Exchange Program
description: The University of Stellenbosch is a bilingual university-Afrikaans and English. Courses may be taught in either language. Students can enroll in regular university courses and international programs taught in English. Students live in university residence halls.

title: UPAEP, Puebla, Mexico
description: A semester or academic year exchange to UPAEP in Puebla, Mexico. Students can take course for international students and regular university courses. Service-learning is possible. Students live with host families.

title: Study Abroad Orientation
description: This course will provide students with tools to understand the logistical, academic, and cross-cultural issues they must navigate during study abroad. Students will engage in activities geared towards learning about the host country and increasing their cultural awareness, cross-cultural sensitivity, and adaptability to a new culture.

title: Directed Studies
description: A scholarly investigation of a subject appropriate to the country in which the student is studying. Directed by a member of the UW-Eau Claire resident faculty and assisted by a local resource person.

title: Independent Study - Juniors
description: Individual project under the direction of a faculty member.

title: International Education Internship
description: Pre-professional internships intended to provide students with concrete, applicable experience in the field of International Education. A complete description of all internships is available in the Center for International Education.

title: Independent Study - Seniors
description: Individual project under the direction of a faculty member.

title: Directed Studies
description: A scholarly investigation of a subject appropriate to the country in which the student is studying. Directed by a member of the UW-Eau Claire resident faculty and assisted by a local resource person.

title: International Education Internship
description: Pre-professional internships intended to provide students with concrete, applicable experience in the field of International Education. A complete description of all internships is available in the Center for International Education.

title: USAC-University of Pau, France Summer
description: Students attend University of Pau, France, through the University Studies Abroad Consortium (USAC), where they will study French language and culture for four or eight weeks. Students live in the dormitories or with a host family.

title: Study Abroad USAC University of Pau, France
description: Students attend University of Pau, France, through the University Studies Abroad Consortium (USAC), where they will study French language and culture. Students may choose to live in the university dormitories or with a host family.

title: USAC Cuba Short Term
description: A 3-6 week summer program or 3 week Winterim program located in Habana, Cuba. Students enroll in a variety of Cuban culture and Central American history and politics courses. Some excursions are included and optional excursions are offered. Students live in bed and breakfast style apartments.

title: USAC Lyon, France Short-Term
description: Students attend Catholic University of Lyon, through the University Studies Abroad Consortium (USAC), where they will study French language and culture. Students may choose to live in the university dormitories or with a host family.

title: USAC-Lyon, France Semester
description: Students attend University of Lyon 2, through the University Studies Abroad Consortium (USAC), where they will study French language and culture. Students may choose to live in the university dormitories or with a host family.

title: USAC: Prague, Czech Republic
description: Study in Prague for the semester or academic year, taking courses in English, about European politics, culture and art, along with a Czech conversation and culture class. Courses are hosted at Charles University and offered through the University Studies Abroad Consortium (USAC). Some field trips are included, and volunteer opportunities may be available, particularly for education students. You’ll live in shared apartments with other USAC students.

title: USAC Costa Rica, Universidad Nacional de Costa Rica
description: Students attend the Universidad Nacional de Costa Rica, through the University Studies Abroad Consortium (USAC), where they will study Spanish language and culture. Students live with host families.

title: USAC Costa Rica, Universidad Nacional de Costa Rica - Summer
description: Students attend the Universidad Nacional de Costa Rica, through the University Studies Abroad Consortium (USAC), where they will study Spanish language and culture. Students live with host families.

title: USAC: Prague, Czech Republic-Short Term
description: Study in Prague for the summer or winterim and take courses, in English, about European politics, culture and art. Courses are hosted at Charles University and offered through the University Studies Abroad Consortium (USAC). Some field trips are included, and an optional 1-credit field study in Vienna and Budapest is offered. Volunteer opportunities may be available, particularly for education students. You’ll live in shared apartments with other USAC students.

title: USAC-Australia: Deakin University-Winterim
description: Live what you are learning! Spend Winterim in Australia, taking a course about contemporary Australia while interacting with Australian university students. This program is offered through the University Studies Abroad Consortium (USAC) and hosted by Deakin University’s Geelong campus. Some social activities are included in the program, and many others are offered for an additional fee. You’ll live in shared apartments.

title: Study Abroad-Limerick, Ireland
description: A one or two semester study abroad program with the University of Limerick in Limerick, Ireland. Students may choose from all classes for which they have appropriate prerequisites. Students live in university residence halls.

title: University of Limerick Int'l Summer School
description: Take a business, healthcare, Irish studies, or law class in this three-week summer school hosted by the University of Limerick! You’ll live in student residences. Organized fieldtrips are included.

title: USAC: Bengaluru, India
description: Study in Bengaluru (Bangalore), India for a semester or the full academic year! You’ll choose from courses taught, in English, in subjects such as Indian culture, global perspectives, Buddhism, health care, gender studies, and Hindi language. Courses are hosted at Christ University and offered through the University Studies Abroad Consortium (USAC). Some field trips are included, and volunteer opportunities are available. You’ll live in co-ed apartments or a women’s only residence hall with other USAC students.

title: USAC: Bengaluru, India-Summer
description: Spend the summer studying in Bengaluru (Bangalore), India! You’ll choose from courses, taught in English, in subjects such as Indian culture, Buddhism, health care, gender studies, and Hindi language. Courses are hosted at Christ University and offered through the University Studies Abroad Consortium (USAC). Some field trips are included, and volunteer opportunities are available. You’ll live in shared apartments with other USAC students.

title: USAC Thailand, Chiang Mai University Summer
description: A four-nine week, summer study abroad program at the USAC Center located in Chiang Mai, Thailand. Students enroll in a variety of Thai culture courses, taught in English. Students live in off campus apartments arranged by USAC.

title: USAC Thailand, Chiang Mai University Semester
description: A one or two semester study abroad program at the USAC Center located on Chiang Mai University. Students enroll in a variety of Thai culture courses, taught in English. Students live in off campus apartments arranged by USAC.

title: USAC-Australia: Deakin University-Semester
description: This program is offered through the University Studies Abroad Consortium (USAC) and hosted by Deakin University in Melbourne, Australia. Students will be fully immersed in the local Australian lifestyle! Enroll in courses amongst local Australian students in a large variety of subject areas. Students live in shared apartments. Optional excursions/activities available.

title: University of Winchester, England
description: Spring semester study abroad program with the University of Winchester in Winchester, England. Students may choose from all classes for which they have appropriate prerequisites. Students live in residence halls on or near campus.

title: USAC China, Sichuan Normal University
description: A five-ten week summer or 3-week Winterim study abroad program at the USAC Center located in Chengdu, China. Students enroll in intensive Chinese language courses and/or Chinese culture courses, taught in English. Students live in university residence hall.

title: USAC China, Sichuan Normal University
description: A one or two semester study abroad program at the USAC Center located in Chengdu, China. Students enroll in either the appropriate level of Chinese Language Studies or select from a variety of Chinese Studies courses taught in English. Students live in university residence halls or apartments.

title: Kansai Gaidai University, Hirakata, Japan-Summer
description: In this six-week summer program, you will take a Japanese language course at an appropriate level and Introduction to Asian Studies, while participating in cultural activities organized by Kansai Gaidai University. Housing is in the international student dormitory on campus.

title: Southern Cross University, Lismore Australia, Summer
description: A Summer program at Southern Cross University. Students may choose from all classes for which they have appropriate prerequisites. Housing is in university apartments.

title: Study Abroad-Murdoch University-Perth, Australia
description: Students can attend courses at Murdoch University during either semester or for the calendar year. Students may choose from a comprehensive curriculum, and take any courses for which they have met the appropriate prerequisites. Housing is in the university apartments.

title: USAC: Israel, University of Haifa-Summer
description: Spend your summer at the University of Haifa, Israel through the University Studies Abroad Consortium (USAC). Choose from intensive Arabic language, intensive Hebrew language, or a Mediterranean studies course. Some field trips are included, and volunteer opportunities may be available. You’ll live in university residence halls.

title: Southern Cross University-Lismore, Australia
description: A semester or calendar year study abroad program with Southern Cross University. Students may choose from all classes for which they have appropriate prerequisites. Housing is in university apartments.

title: USAC: Israel, University of Haifa
description: Study for the semester or an academic year at the University of Haifa, Israel through the University Studies Abroad Consortium (USAC). Choose from regular university courses, taught in English, in areas such as communications, Israel/Middle East studies, peace/conflict studies, history, literature, religious studies, and Arabic and Hebrew language. Spring semester students can go early for an intensive language class. Some field trips are included, and volunteer opportunities may be available. You’ll live in university residence halls.

title: USAC Thailand, Khon Kaen University Semester
description: A one or two semester study abroad program at Khon Kaen University (KKU), an education, healthcare, and technology hub located in northeastern Thailand. Students enroll in Thai language courses while taking English-taught courses in tourism, media technology (audio, film, animation), international affairs, and international journalism. Students are housed in KKU on-campus residence halls or off-campus private apartments.

title: USAC Thailand, Khon Kaen Summer
description: A ten-week study abroad program at the USAC Center in Khon Kaen, Thailand, an education, healthcare, and technology hub located in northeastern Thailand. Students enroll in mandatory Intro to Thai Language and Healthcare in Thailand courses for the first two weeks of the program followed by an 8 week internship program with the addition of optional electives. Students are housed in on-campus residence halls or off-campus private apartments.

title: USAC New Zealand: Massey University Wellington-Summer
description: Students choose one of the three tracks for the National Expedition Field Study in Wellington, New Zealand (15 Massey credits= 3UWEC): (communication & marketing; disaster risk & emergency management; creative expression & digital media). Then the last 4 weeks they complete an internship (15 Massey credits= 3 UWEC) experience at the end of the program. Field study taught by local faculty and with local and international students.

title: USAC New Zealand: Massey University Hawke's Bay-Summer
description: Spend your summer on the National Expedition Field Study: Agriculture & Environment in Hawke's Bay, New Zealand. Enroll in 15 Massey credits (3 UWEC) for the first two weeks of program. Then the last 4 weeks, complete an internship placement for 15 Massey credits (3 UWEC). This program is only for those pursuing environmental/agriculture focused degrees. Field study is taught by local faculty and with other international/local students.

title: USAC Japan, Nagasaki University of Foreign Studies
description: A study abroad program for one semester or the academic year in the historic city of Nagasaki. Students take Japanese language courses and Asian Studies courses taught in English. Students live with Japanese families or in university residence halls.

title: Study Abroad-Harlaxton College, England
description: A one-semester program at Harlaxton College near Grantham, England, offered through an arrangement with the University of Evansville. Courses are offered by British and U.S. faculty at Harlaxton College. INTS 170 or INTS 370, British Life and Civilization, is required. Students and faculty live in the Harlaxton Manor or Carriage House.

title: Study Abroad-Harlaxton, England Summer
description: Summer program at Harlaxton College near Grantham, England, in partnership with the University of Evansville. Courses taught by British and U.S. faculty. Housing is in Harlaxton Manor or Carriage House.

title: Southern Cross University-Gold Coast, Australia
description: A semester or calendar year study abroad program with Southern Cross University. Students may choose from all classes for which they have appropriate prerequisites. Housing is in university apartments.

title: University of Aberdeen, Aberdeen, Scotland
description: A one or two semester study abroad program with the University of Aberdeen in Aberdeen, Scotland. Students may choose from all classes for which they have appropriate prerequisites. Students live in university residence halls.

title: University of Glasgow, Glasgow, Scotland
description: A one or two semester study abroad program with the University of Glasgow in Glasgow, Scotland. Students may choose from all classes for which they have appropriate prerequisites. Students live in university residence halls.

title: University of Glasgow International Summer School
description: The U of Glasgow International Summer School provides a number of STEM options in biology, geology, mathematics and physics. Students may participate in a June session, a July session, or both, depending upon course choice. Housing is in university residence halls.

title: USAC: Reggio Emilio, Italy
description: Study for a semester or the academic year in Reggio Emilia, Italy! You’ll take an intensive Italian language course, then choose from courses, taught in English, in education, health, communications and Italian studies. Courses are hosted at the University of Modena and Reggio Emilia (UNIMORE) and offered through the University Studies Abroad Consortium (USAC). Classes include visits to relevant local organizations including schools, hospitals, and local businesses. Internships may also be available for qualified students. Some field trips are included, and optional excursions are offered at additional cost. Most students live in residence halls; limited homestay options are available.

title: USAC: Reggio Emilio, Italy-Summer
description: Spend your summer in Reggio Emilia, Italy! Choose from courses, taught in English, about education, health, communications and Italian language and culture. Courses are hosted at the University of Modena and Reggio Emilia (UNIMORE) and offered by the University Studies Abroad Consortium (USAC). Classes include visits to relevant local organizations including schools, hospitals, and local businesses. Internships may also be available for qualified students attending both sessions. Some field trips are included, and optional excursions are offered at additional cost. Most students live in residence halls; limited homestay options are available.

title: USAC: Bilbao, Spain
description: A one or two semester program at the USAC Center located on the University of the Basque Country (UPV). Enroll in Spanish language study and electives, taught in Spanish or English, in language, literature, business or cultural studies. No previous Spanish required. Students live in off campus homestays or apartments arranged by USAC. Some excursions included.

title: USAC: Bilbao, Spain-Summer
description: A three-eight week, summer program at the USAC Center located on the University of the Basque Country (UPV). Students can enroll in intensive language courses or choose from a variety of electives, taught in English, in business and cultural studies. Students live in shared apartments or a homestay. Some excursions included.

title: USAC: Valencia, Spain
description: A one or two semester program at the USAC Center located at the Polytechnic University of Valencia. Enroll in a Spanish language track and electives, taught in Spanish or English, literature, business, STEM or cultural studies. No previous Spanish required. Students live in off campus shared apartments or homestays arranged by USAC. Some excursions included.

title: USAC: Valencia, Spain-Summer
description: A three-eight week, summer program at the USAC Center located on the Polytechnic University of Valencia. Students can enroll in intensive language courses or choose from electives, taught in English, in science, engineering, math and business. Students live in shared apartments or a homestay. Some excursions included.

title: University of Valladolid, Spain-Summer
description: The University of Valladolid offers a number of summer program options, including intensive language and culture at all levels, Spanish for business, Spanish for healthcare, and a seminar on teaching Spanish as a foreign language for students about to start their student teaching. Cultural excursions are included in the program; students live with host families.

title: Study Abroad-Valladolid, Spain
description: The University of Valladolid offers an intensive Spanish language and culture program for U.S. and other international students. UW-Eau Claire participants earn 12 to 16 credits toward their Spanish major or minor. Students live with host families.

title: USAC Poland Semester, Cracow University of Economics
description: A one or two semester study abroad program at the USAC center located in Kraków, Poland. Students will enroll in a variety of Polish culture, European studies, international business and Jewish studies courses. All courses are taught in English. Student live in off campus apartments or residence halls arranged by USAC.

title: USAC Poland Summer, Cracow University of Economics
description: Spend the summer at the USAC center located in Kraków, Poland. Students will enroll in a variety of Polish culture, European studies, international business and Jewish studies/Holocaust studies courses. Students will live in off campus apartments or residence halls. Some excursions included.

title: USAC South Korea: Chonnam National University
description: Students attending Chonnam National University may enroll in a wide variety of courses, taught in English. Housing is in double rooms within a campus residence hall. There is an opportunity for students to be paired with a local Korean student buddy to practice Korean/English. No previous language is required.

title: USAC South Korea: Chonnam National University-Summer
description: Spend 4 weeks and take up to 6 credits in a wide variety of courses in a wide variety of majors at CNU's International Summer Session. Courses are taught by local faculty and are with other international students. Some field trips included.

title: USAC-South Korea: Yonsei University Short-Term
description: Spend summer or winterim at Yonsei University in Seoul, South Korea. Both terms offer a selection of courses, taught in English. Students who participate in the 3 week winterim program can enroll in 3-6 credits. In the 6-7 week summer program, students enroll in 3-9 credits. Summer students can also participate in an internship program. Included and optional field trips are offered both terms!

title: USAC-South Korea: Yonsei University-Semester
description: This program is offered through the University Studies Abroad Consortium (USAC) and hosted by Yonsei University in Seoul, South Korea. Students will be fully immersed in the Korean culture as they enroll in courses amongst local and international students. A large variety of courses are taught in English. Students live in residence halls. Optional excursions/activities available.

title: USAC Universidade Catolica Portuguesa, Lisbon, Portugal-Summer
description: In this four-or-eight week summer program, you will choose from business, economics and Portuguese studies courses taught in English. UCP is in the 1% of universities worldwide with "Triple Crown" business accreditation (AACSB, European Foundation for Management Development, Association for MBAs). You'll live in student housing with shared kitchen, bath and living facilities. Cultural activities are available.

title: USAC: Torino, Italy
description: Study for a semester or the academic year in Torino, Italy! You’ll take an intensive Italian language course, then choose from courses, taught in English, in business, design, digital marketing and social media management, health and wellness, and Italian studies. Courses are hosted at the SAA - School of Management of the University of Torino and offered through the University Studies Abroad Consortium (USAC). Classes include visits to relevant local organizations including sports stadiums and local businesses. Internships may also be available for qualified students. Some field trips are included, and optional excursions are offered at additional cost. Students live in private apartments shared with other USAC students.

title: USAC: Torino, Italy-Summer
description: Spend your summer in Torino, Italy! You’ll take an intensive Italian language course, then choose from courses, taught in English, in business, design, digital marketing and social media management, health and wellness, and Italian studies. Courses are hosted at the SAA - School of Management of the University of Torino and offered through the University Studies Abroad Consortium (USAC). Classes include visits to relevant local organizations including sports stadiums and local businesses. Internships may also be available for qualified students. Some field trips are included, and optional excursions are offered at additional cost. Students live in private apartments shared with other USAC students.

title: USAC: Verona, Italy
description: Study for a semester or the academic year in Verona, Italy! You’ll take an intensive Italian language course, then choose from courses, taught in English, in international business, tourism, and Italian studies. Courses are hosted at the Verona campus of IUSVE University (Salesian University Institute of Venice) and offered through the University Studies Abroad Consortium (USAC). Classes may include visits to relevant local organizations and local businesses. Internships may also be available for qualified students. Some field trips are included, and optional excursions are offered at additional cost. Students live in apartment-style residence halls shared with other USAC students.

title: USAC: Verona, Italy-Summer
description: Spend your summer in Verona, Italy! You’ll take an intensive Italian language course, then choose from courses, taught in English, in international business, tourism, and Italian studies. Courses are hosted at the Verona campus of IUSVE University (Salesian University Institute of Venice) and offered through the University Studies Abroad Consortium (USAC). Classes may include visits to relevant local organizations and local businesses. Internships may also be available for qualified students. Some field trips are included, and optional excursions are offered at additional cost. Students live in apartment-style residence halls shared with other USAC students.

title: University of Stirling, Stirling, Scotland
description: A one or two semester study abroad program with the University of Stirling in Stirling, Scotland. Students may choose from all classes for which they have appropriate prerequisites. Students live in university residence halls.

title: Chile, Pontificia Universidad Católica de Valparaíso
description: Students attending the Pontificia Universidad Católica de Valparaíso take Spanish language courses, along with regular university offerings. Students live with host families.

title: University of Stirling, Stirling, Scotland
description: A four or eight week, summer study abroad program at the University of Stirling in Scotland. Students take one to three classes for a total of three to nine credits. Internship opportunities exist. Students live in residence halls.

title: USAC New Zealand, Massey University
description: Students attending Massey University can enroll in regular university courses. There are three campuses that students can attend through this program. Students will live in university residence halls.

title: University of Stellenbosch, South Africa
description: A four-week summer program at the University of Stellenbosch. Students take one course in South African political history and choose from three tracks: African studies, business, or public health. Students live in university residence halls.

title: Stellenbosch University, South Africa
description: The University of Stellenbosch is a bilingual university—Afrikaans and English. Courses may be taught in either language. Students can enroll in regular university courses and international programs taught in English. Students live in university residence halls.

title: Chile, Pontificia Universidad Católica de Valparaíso, Winterim
description: Students choose 2 classes (six credits) from the PUC-V intensive Spanish program and participate in co-curricular cultural activities. Housing is with host families.

title: Marburg, Germany
description: The Intensive German Studies Program (IGSP) and International Undergraduate Studies Program (IUSP) at the University of Marburg, Germany immerse students in German language and culture. IUSP students also take regular university classes in English or German. Regional excursions are included. Students live in residence halls.

title: USAC Portugal, Universidade Catolica Portuguesa, Lisbon Semester
description: A one or two semester program on the Universidade Catolica Portuguesa campus. Enroll in Portuguese language study and electives, taught in Portuguese or English. Academic areas also include European studies; culture, society, and politics.

title: Lorenzo de' Medici Florence
description: Lorenzo de' Medici Florence offers a wide range of courses in English. Housing is in student apartments. This program is for the Fall or Spring semester or academic year.

title: Lorenzo de' Medici Tuscania Italy
description: Lorenzo de' Medici Tuscania offers a limited number of humanities and social science courses in English. Housing is in student apartments or with host families. This program is for the Fall or Spring semester or academic year.

title: Lorenzo de' Medici: Two Italies
description: The Lorenzo de' Medici Two Italies program includes six weeks each in Tuscania and Florence, with a set curriculum of three credits of Italian language and four courses in English. Housing in apartments. This program is for the Fall or Spring semester.

title: Lorenzo de' Medici Florence
description: Lorenzo de' Medici Florence offers a wide range of courses in English. Housing is in student apartments. This program is for the Winterim term or Summer term.

title: Lorenzo de' Medici Tuscania Italy
description: Lorenzo de' Medici Tuscania offers a limited number of humanities and social science courses in English. Housing is in student apartments or with host families. This program is for a four-week Summer term.

title: UPAEP, Puebla, Mexico Summer
description: A six-week Spanish language summer program with a number of program tracks including business, education, healthcare and intensive Spanish. Many tracks include a practical experience in the community. Housing is with host families.

title: Ajou University, South Korea-Summer
description: Students attend courses scheduled at Ajou University's International Summer School. Courses are typically offered in business and economics, Korean language, and social sciences. Students live in university residence halls.

title: USAC: Montevideo, Uruguay, Universidad ORT
description: A one or two semester program at the USAC center located at the Universidad ORT. Enroll in a Spanish language track, Spanish elective courses and take courses in activism and gender, Latin American studies, business, viticulture and agribusiness. Students live with a host family arranged by USAC. Some excursions are included.

title: USAC: Montevideo, Uruguay - Short term
description: A short term program located in Montevideo, Uruguay. Students can enroll in a variety of classes for Spanish language and literature studies and Latin American and Gender studies. Some excursions are included. Accommodations are with host families or in apartments in a residence hall.

title: Virtual International Internship Experience
description: Complete a virtual internship, with an international organization/company, from the comforts of your own home! Placements will be with companies based outside the U.S. and be tailored to the student's academic/professional interests. Specific placements cannot be guaranteed. All internships are unpaid.

title: API International Internships
description: This program gives students the opportunity to intern in 9 different countries. Housing will vary depending on location. Students will be placed in an internship that best fits their academic and career goals. The program is offered in the summer and is 8 weeks long. Internships are unpaid.

title: Beginning Japanese I
description: A foundation in listening, speaking, reading, writing, and culture. Introduction to hiragana, katakana, and some kanji characters.

title: Beginning Japanese II
description: Continuation of Japanese 101. Study of useful conversation patterns, grammar, and culture. Introduction to Kanji-character (91) writing.

title: Intermediate Japanese I
description: Review of Japanese grammar and vocabulary with focus on the development of reading, writing, speaking, and listening skills. Students gain an understanding of Japanese culture through a variety of topics.

title: Intermediate Japanese II
description: Continuation of Japanese 201.

title: Advanced Japanese Language and Culture I
description: Designed to improve speaking, listening, reading, and writings skills. Students gain an understanding of Japanese culture and current events through a variety of topics.

title: Advanced Japanese Language and Culture II
description: Continuation of Japanese 301. Focuses on improving speaking, listening comprehension, reading, and writing modern Japanese. Extensive use of original spoken and written materials. Further knowledge of sociolinguistics/culture and additional kanji. Emphasis on accurate and appropriate communication.

title: Specialty
description: Special class activity to be offered as expertise is available.

title: Intercollegiate Athletics
description: The student will participate in the physical activity and lectures pertaining to the preparation of a varsity athletic team for a season and the contests included in that season.

title: Wellness Concepts and Health Related Fitness
description: Students will work to develop health-related fitness and will learn the importance of wellness concepts such as health-related fitness, stress management, nutrition, and consumer responsibility in health and fitness.

title: Orientation to Kinesiology
description: Introduction to disciplines and professions in Kinesiology. Students will become familiar with the possible options available for majors and minors as well as potential careers compatible with a Kinesiology degree.

title: Prevention and Care of Athletic/Exercise Injuries
description: This lecture/lab based course is an introduction to the basic concepts and fundamentals of the prevention, recognition, treatment, and rehabilitation of athletic or exercise-related injuries.

title: Introduction to Physical Education
description: Introduction to physical education including understanding the teaching/learning process, effective teaching skills, and context and reflection.

title: Management and Coaching Theory
description: An introduction to the coaching profession including current management principles and practices.

title: Anatomical Kinesiology
description: Introduction to human anatomy and the basic structure of the body's major systems. An emphasis will be placed on the musculoskeletal system and an understanding of its relationship to human movement.

title: Biomechanical Kinesiology
description: The relationship of motion, kinematics, kinetics, and fluid mechanics to human movement.

title: Exercise Physiology
description: The physiological basis of human movement and the functional changes in body systems as a result of exercise.

title: Introduction to Exercise Science
description: Introduction to the foundational concepts associated with physical activity, fitness, and health that underlie the advanced study of exercise prescription and programming.

title: Psychology of Sport and Physical Activity
description: The course will examine psychological aspects which affect peak performance in sport, along with exploring the effect that sport and physical activity have on individuals.

title: Sociology of Sport and Physical Activity
description: Introduction to the social influences on sport and physical activity as well as the influence sport has on society.

title: Foundations of Rehabilitation Science
description: Introduction to the historical, philosophical, and theoretical foundations of rehabilitation science. The course also provides information on current trends and issues in rehabilitation science research.

title: Physical Education Curriculum Models and Teaching Styles
description: This class will guide you in understanding the essential curriculum models and teaching styles that shape the field of physical education. You will be challenged to deepen your pedagogical knowledge while building the skills to apply innovative instructional strategies in your own teaching practice. Additionally, the course will encourage you to think critically about how to design and deliver developmentally appropriate, engaging, and effective learning experiences.

title: Measurement and Assessment in Physical Education
description: Prospective physical education teachers in this course will develop the knowledge, dispositions, and performance competencies necessary to effectively measure and assess student learning in K–12 physical education.

title: Teaching Fundamental Motor Skills in Physical Education
description: This course is designed to help you integrate and apply the knowledge and skills you have gained in the PETE program so far, while also pushing you to expand your thinking and grow as an educator. We will explore pedagogical knowledge and teaching strategies used for teaching fundamental motor skills to elementary aged children. You will also develop a strong understanding of the skill themes approach and learn how to design developmentally appropriate instruction that supports student growth and engagement. This course will also provide hands-on teaching experiences through our Homeschool PE program, giving you authentic opportunities to practice and refine your teaching in real-world settings.

title: Fitness Concepts in Physical Education
description: Prospective teachers completing this course will acquire the dispositions, knowledge, and performance competencies related to appropriate health-related fitness of school-aged children in K-12 physical education. Opportunity to earn SHAPE America’s Physical Best Specialist certificate.

title: Team and Individual Activities in Physical Education
description: This class will guide you in understanding how to plan, implement, and assess instruction across a wide range of team and individual activities, including invasion, net/wall, target, and striking and fielding. You will be expanding beyond your ability to design lessons and units that align with k-12 PE standards; the course will encourage you to use your knowledge and imagination to create a semester-long Project-Based Learning (PBL) experience that integrates these activities in meaningful and innovative ways.

title: Teaching Lifetime Activities in Physical Education
description: This class will guide you in understanding a wide range of lifetime activities, including rhythms and dance, adventure education, swimming, yoga, and outdoor pursuits such as hiking, cross country skiing, and snowshoeing. You will be developing the skills and confidence to plan, implement, and adapt instruction across these activities while aligning lessons with SHAPE America standards. Additionally, the course will encourage you to think critically about how lifetime activities foster creativity, resilience, social-emotional learning, and lifelong engagement in physical activity through your own participation.

title: Introduction to School Health Education and Current Health Issues
description: A theory-and activity-based course in which students will discover the importance of health education in the schools and explore current health issues surrounding our nation's youth.

title: Strategies and Assessment in School Health Education
description: This course will focus on using experiential learning techniques and developing authentic assessments to be used in school health education classes. Student completes approximately 10-15 hours of observation/teaching.

title: Health Education Curriculum Design & Practicum
description: Students will design and evaluate the effectiveness of School Health Education Curricula. Students will plan effective comprehensive health instruction with special emphasis on making curricular connections to effectively integrate health content into multiple disciplines. Student completes approximately 75 hours of observation/teaching.

title: Academic Apprenticeship in Kinesiology
description: Experience for qualified students, supervised by Kinesiology faculty, to facilitate teaching and learning in a specific course. Students will enhance their knowledge of the discipline and their ability to communicate this understanding to their peers.

title: Coaching Apprenticeship
description: Experience for qualified students, selected and supervised by faculty members, to facilitate coaching and learning in a coaching setting. Students enhance their knowledge and their ability to communicate that knowledge to other students.

title: Exercise Science Apprenticeship
description: Experience for qualified students, selected and supervised by faculty members, to facilitate teaching and learning in an exercise science setting. Students enhance their knowledge and their ability to communicate that knowledge to other students.

title: Apprentice in Teaching Physical Education
description: Experience for qualified students, selected and supervised by faculty members, to facilitate teaching and learning in a specific course. Students enhance their knowledge and their ability to communicate that knowledge to other students.

title: Cancer Recovery and Fitness Lab
description: Lecture/ Experience for qualified students, selected and supervised by faculty members, to facilitate applied experiential learning in the field of exercise and cancer recovery. Students enhance their knowledge and ability to communicate that knowledge to others.

title: Cancer Recovery and Fitness Seminar
description: A seminar experience designed for students interested in the area of exercise rehabilitation with cancer survivorship. Students will gain knowledge related to cancer treatment and side-effects, the benefits and fundamentals of exercise principles in cancer recovery, and will gain valuable information related to the survivorship issues currently dealt with in the Cancer Recovery and Fitness community outreach program.

title: Laboratory Procedures in Exercise Science
description: A theory and laboratory course of the measurement and analysis of the physiology tests for health, performance, and research commonly used in an exercise physiology laboratory.

title: Exercise Physiology Laboratory Assessment
description: A theory and lab course of the measurement and analysis of the physiology tests for health and performance commonly used in a human performance lab.

title: Essentials of Strength and Conditioning
description: The purpose of this course is to introduce the student to strength and conditioning program design, implementation and evaluation. It is also intended to prepare students for the practical component of the National Strength & Conditioning Association (NSCA) Certified Strength & Conditioning Specialist (CSCS) examination.

title: Fitness Assessment and Exercise Prescription
description: This course provides students with the theoretical knowledge and practical skills required of the entry-level exercise specialist.

title: Special Topics
description: Special topic in kinesiology.

title: Motor Development Across the Lifespan
description: A study of changes in motor behavior across the lifespan, the processes that underlie these changes, and factors that affect them.

title: Rehabilitation Science Seminar
description: The primary purpose of this course is to provide students with an opportunity to review the critical knowledge, skills, and abilities developed throughout the curriculum and to reflect on their undergraduate experience.

title: Basic Electrocardiography
description: This course is designed to fill the needs of students who desire the ability to interpret the resting, normal, and abnormal ECG, as well as provide an overview of cardiac anatomy, function, and neurophysiology.

title: Applied Nutrition in Kinesiology
description: The purpose of this course is to gain insight into nutritional issues related to various populations. Students will assess dietary, physical, and performance standards related to these populations and develop alternative plans for healthy lifestyles.

title: Clinical Exercise Physiology
description: This course will focus on the prevention and management of chronic diseases through exercise intervention.

title: Community Fitness Programming
description: Prescribing and leading exercise in the early morning fitness program which includes community members with a wide variety of physical capabilities.

title: Research Methods in Kinesiology I
description: This course is the first half of the research methods course. It is designed to address the fundamental aspects of conducting systematic hypothesis-driven research in human health, kinesiology, and performance using the scientific method. The class will be separated into groups (4-5 students/group). Each group will work together and under the supervision of a Kinesiology Faculty advisor to design and propose a research project of your choice over the course of the semester. Group responsibility will include formulating novel research questions and hypotheses; locating, reading, and interpreting peer-reviewed scientific studies; developing group-based research projects; and submitting the proposal to the Institutional Review Board for an approval to collect data during KINS 473 Research Methods in Kinesiology II.

title: Research Methods in Kinesiology II
description: This course is the second part of the Research Methods course and will address the fundamental aspects of conducting systematic hypothesis-driven research in human health, kinesiology, and performance using the scientific method. The class will be separated into groups (4-5 students/group). Each group will work together and under the supervision of a Kinesiology Faculty advisor to design and complete a research project of your choice over the course of the semester. Group responsibility for this portion of the research methods course will include data collection, statistical analyses and interpretation, writing a research manuscript, and preparing an oral presentation.

title: Exercise Science Seminar
description: This is the capstone experience for students in Exercise Science. Students will develop a portfolio, lead and participate in professional discussions and present a project.

title: Research Methods in Kinesiology I
description: This course is the first half of the research methods course. It is designed to address the fundamental aspects of conducting systematic hypothesis-driven research in human health, kinesiology, and performance using scientific methods. Group responsibility will include formulating novel research questions and hypotheses; locating, reading, and interpreting peer-reviewed scientific studies; developing group-based research projects; and submitting the proposal to the Institutional Review Board for an approval to collect data during KINS 478 Research Methods in Kinesiology II.

title: Research Methods in Kinesiology II
description: This course is the second part of the Research Methods course and will address the fundamental aspects of conducting systematic hypothesis-driven research in human health, kinesiology, and performance using the scientific method. Group responsibility for this portion of the research methods course will include data collection, statistical analyses and interpretation, writing a research manuscript, and preparing an oral presentation.

title: Adapted Physical Activity
description: A theory and lab course examining exceptionalities as they apply to the physical education environment. Study includes legal implications, IEPs, individual differences, assessment principles, modification of tasks and learning environments, and field experiences (30 hours).

title: Assessment in Adapted Physical Education
description: A theory and lab course examining the appropriate assessment tools and procedures for special populations in physical education.

title: Practicum in Rehabilitation Science
description: A lab course applying knowledge and practical skills in a full-time rehabilitation work environment. Field experience a minimum of 100 hours is required.

title: Coaching Practicum and Seminar
description: A capstone experience where the student develops a coaching portfolio. Seminar focuses on special topics in sport. Field experience of a minimum of 60 hours will apply teaching, administrative, and management skills in coaching.

title: Practicum in Adapted Physical Education
description: A lab course applying principles of programming and assessment in the special physical education environment. Field experience a minimum of 75 hours is required.

title: Directed Studies
description: Study, under the immediate direction of a kinesiology faculty member, of a problem or special area not covered in existing courses.

title: Capstone-Teaching Physical Education
description: Students will integrate and apply their knowledge, skills, and dispositions to case studies and issues in physical education teaching.

title: Exercise Science Internship
description: Students are provided with an opportunity to integrate and apply the knowledge, skills, and abilities developed through their undergraduate education in a professional setting.

title: Independent Study
description: Individual research project under the direction of a faculty member.
