title: Understanding a Diverse Workforce
description: Prepares students to manage a diverse workforce.

title: Introduction to Assurance Services
description: This course helps students identify situations in which assurance services are necessary, evaluate different types of assurance services and assess the quality of assurance services companies and investment funds provide.

title: Introduction to Financial Fraud
description: This course introduces you to occupational fraud. You will learn how to identify and prevent fraud, addressing issues such as fraudulent financial reporting and misappropriation of assets, which are significant problems for businesses of all sizes. You will gain valuable knowledge about how fraud is committed and how to spot the potential for fraud in an organization.

title: Tax Impact of Business Decisions
description: This course introduces the student to an overall framework for evaluating the impact that taxes have on business decisions. While the course concentrates on the impact of federal income taxes, the concepts are general and can apply to a variety of taxing jurisdictions.

title: Mutual Fund Investing
description: You will learn how to evaluate mutual funds, build diversified portfolios tailored to investor demographics, and assess mutual fund and portfolio performance effectively. This course develops your personal finance expertise, helping you make informed investment decisions that contribute to your overall financial well-being.

title: Financial Analysis for Strategic Decision Making
description: You will gain an in-depth understanding of key financial management principles and practices, with a focus on honing the ability to apply financial tools and techniques needed for strategic leadership. Building on this foundation, you will develop practical skills with an emphasis on performance evaluation, financial decision making, and an understanding of the ethical considerations in corporate finance.

title: Investment Analysis and Management
description: This course will develop a thorough understanding of the portfolio construction thought process. Students will discover how to manage a portfolio by examining the logic and potential of using options and futures to enhance return and minimize risk.

title: Incorporation of Exchange Rates in Strategic Decision-Making
description: Students will acquire competency with the terminology, strategies, and practices needed to interact with confidence in business settings that require working within the global currency market.

title: Regional Trade Agreements and Multinational Corporations
description: This course introduces students to issues relating to regional trade agreements (trading blocs), giving special attention to such agreements in the Americas, Asia and Europe. It will also examine how regional trade agreements might affect Multinational Corporations (MNCs).

title: Business in East Asia
description: This course provides perspective on business in Japan, South Korea, and China by looking at the historical backgrounds of each market and examining how those backgrounds impact present economic and business relations. Students focus on understanding each country's business practices and learn strategies to build successful East Asian partnerships and to succeed in business in East Asia.

title: Corporate Venturing
description: Upon completing this course, you will be able to evaluate and design a plan for a new venture and make a compelling proposal that can be implemented and championed at your firm. This expertise will enhance your impact on your organization's entrepreneurial initiatives and strategic direction. You will learn how to encourage entrepreneurship within your firm, capitalize on new business opportunities, set up the planning processes, and select the right managers for the project.

title: Fundamental Methods for Forecasting
description: Forecasting trends using regularly refreshed data is crucial in business. With the power of modern computers, it is possible to carry out effective short-term (one or two periods ahead) forecasts using methods studied in the context of this short course. The course will be heavily weighted toward applied work rather than the mathematical theory behind the methods. Students will have a portfolio of forecasts at the end of the course to demonstrate competence as well as a solid understanding of forecasting terminology.

title: Consumer Behavior
description: This class will help you understand why consumers behave as they do. Using consumer behavior theories and our own purchase practices, we will attempt to predict how consumers will respond to a variety of marketing activities.

title: Managing Workplace Stress
description: Workplace stress costs millions of dollars annually in lost productivity and turnover. We will examine theories, causes, and consequences of stress and take a hands-on approach to address workplace stress.

title: Strategies and Tactics of Pricing
description: This course presents a framework for evaluating and making pricing decisions. Pricing is examined from the perspective of customers, competitors and costs; in a variety of contexts, including business-to-business markets, consumer goods markets, services, and not-for-profit companies.

title: Selling Ideas at Work
description: This course equips students with the skills necessary to effectively sell ideas at work, position themselves for more wins, and improve their results. Creating more opportunities in the workplace and making impactful, critical decisions helps business professionals reach their goals. By assembling a plan to sell an idea to decision makers, students will learn how to improve their sales skills. Students will apply adaptive selling to various decision making environments through planning, presenting, strategic decision making, and impactful communication.

title: Employee Training and Development
description: In today's competitive global market, enhancing an organization's workforce through an employee development strategy is essential for maintaining a competitive edge. This course will develop your skills in key areas such as needs assessment, workshop design, learning transfer, and employee development. Additionally, the course addresses current trends in employee training, equipping you with the tools to enhance organizational success.

title: Project Management
description: This course will elevate your leadership by mastering essential project management skills. You will learn to select, plan, and manage successful projects to enhance an organization’s strategy. You will develop comprehensive project management plans and learn about common issues affecting project team dynamics. In addition to focusing on the planning process of project management, you will also be introduced to traditional and agile execution methodologies.

title: Organizational Leadership and Change
description: This course is designed to equip professionals with a comprehensive understanding and practical skills in managing and leading organizational change. Beginning with an introduction to the fundamental concepts of change management, students will analyze the significance of change management for organizational success, exploring key principles, historical impacts, types of change, and the dynamic nature of business environments. Through discussions, reflections on personal experiences, and case studies, learners will engage with the material, establishing a solid foundation in change management.

title: Business and Ethics
description: In this course you will develop and apply critical thinking skills to address complex ethical questions in business, including reflection on how cognitive biases and potential blind spots influence decision making in business. In this course you will use ethical frameworks and other tools to analyze ethical questions that are often relevant in the business world, such as, “Does a company have any obligation to help solve social problems?” and “What is a manufacturer's obligation to the consumer with respect to product defects and safety?”

title: Work-Life Challenges and Organizational Practice
description: This course explores the evolving meaning of work-life integration from both employee and employer perspectives. You will critically analyze public policies, organizational practices, and workplace culture while reflecting on your own experiences. Through applied assignments and personal reflection, the course builds toward a deeper understanding of how work-life balance is shaped and how it can be improved.

title: Advanced Topics in Project Management
description: The advanced topics course is oriented toward the more experienced project manager and/or those who supervise project managers. The focus of the course will be to address some current “hot topics” in the field.

title: Fundamentals of Contract Law
description: This course introduces you to the foundational principles of contract law, empowering you to make business decisions that minimize risks and maximize benefits. You will also gain an understanding of legal rights and available remedies for addressing contract breaches and disputes.

title: Project Execution and Control
description: The primary focus of this course is identifying and managing challenges commonly seen in doing the work in projects after the project baseline has been approved.

title: Project Portfolio Management
description: This course is designed to clarify ways to manage the project portfolio which involves tracking and managing all of an organization's projects, keep an eye on costs and resources, comply with deadlines and ensure the projects are in line with the company's overall mission.

title: E-Business Fundamentals for Managers
description: This course provides an overview of the fast-growing Internet-based economy and asks, from a management point of view, How may e-business contribute to the operational, tactical and strategic goals of an organization?

title: Introduction to Enterprise Resource Planning Systems
description: This course describes the principal functions of ERP software and emphasizes the business impacts of such.

title: Communicating for Success: Writing for Results
description: In this course, you’ll strengthen your workplace writing skills by learning how to craft effective good-news/neutral, bad-news, and persuasive messages. You will apply practical strategies directly to documents you write in your role with your current organization, making your learning immediately relevant and applicable. You will also explore workplace communication challenges and refine your writing so that you can write more clearly, confidently, and strategically in any professional setting.

title: Sustainability & Organizational Management
description: This course introduces students to the interrelationship between business and the natural environment, examining the impact of the environment on business and the consequences of business decisions on the environment.

title: Information System Security
description: Today's manager must understand security and risk management. Students will discuss system vulnerabilities, mitigation strategies, and security-related personnel issues. They will be able to develop and implement security procedures and processes.

title: Communicating for Success: Speak, Engage, Lead
description: The purpose of this course is to enhance students' workplace communication skills. Students and their organizations will benefit as students apply practical strategies to oral and interpersonal communication contexts for nearly any audience.

title: Managing Technology in Turbulent Times
description: Managing Technology in Turbulent Times prepares you to lead the organization through rapid technological advancements and dynamic business environments. The course emphasizes strategic alignment, adaptability, and innovation as critical skills for leveraging technology to address uncertainty and drive organizational success. You will analyze emerging trends, assess the role of technology in achieving business goals, and develop strategies to address evolving challenges.

title: E-Business Website Development
description: This course provides students with skills to develop a simple and effective e-business website. It examines design factors and the development process and focuses on facilitation of intra-organizational and inter-organizational communication and extra-organizational relationships.

title: Leading the Healthcare Workforce
description: This course explores challenges and opportunities that leaders in healthcare face. Students will learn how to maximize human resources and other organizational resources to improve organizational efficiencies and the quality of healthcare. Topics include motivation, EDIB (equity, diversity, inclusion, belonging), managing change, and other concepts related to leadership.

title: Fundamentals of Health Information Technology Management
description: This course provides an introduction to the dynamic world of management of information technology in health care. A description of information processing, the content and evaluation of healthcare information systems and the current and future methodologies being employed to acquire and manage information are discussed.

title: Economics of Health and Health Care
description: This course examines the economic forces that shape health care markets and health-related outcomes. You will explore how government intervention, health insurance structures, and provider incentives influence health care costs, access, and the behaviors of individuals. By the end of the course, you will be able to evaluate solutions informed by your own analyses of health economics research and health policy, as well as understand the impact of policy on financial decision-making within healthcare markets.

title: Making and Communicating Data-Driven Decisions in Healthcare
description: In this course you will apply strategies that support organizational decision making including gathering, analyzing, interpreting, and communicating data. You will use storytelling and data visualization to communicate data-driven decisions clearly and accurately for the diverse audiences in healthcare organizations. You will also gain hands-on experience in designing accessible visuals to present data clearly.

title: Supply Chain Management
description: This course covers strategies, concepts, and tools involved in managing supply chain operations, emphasizing qualitative and quantitative tools used in managerial decision-marking. Topics include supply chain strategy, supplier management, inventory management, logistics, and negotiation. You will learn how to create competitive advantages for an organization's approach to supply chain strategy through the concepts covered in this course.

title: Organizational Reward Systems
description: This course covers topics related to intrinsic and extrinsic rewards used to achieve organizations' strategic goals. Topics include: motivational theories, compensation surveys, job evaluation, merit pay, variable or at risk pay, and team compensation.

title: Addressing Contemporary Issues and Trends in Healthcare
description: Students will examine current topics in healthcare in the United States. Topics may include, but are not limited to, healthcare costs; impacts of technology such as electronic records management, telehealth, and artificial intelligence; insurance and reimbursement; staffing; government and other regulatory requirements; equity and access to care; and burnout and stress management. Students will identify trends and issues relevant to their experiences, in particular, and explore strategies for addressing them.

title: Quantitative Business Analysis
description: This course focuses on applied quantitative business analysis and uses Microsoft Excel. The course covers problem solving concepts and techniques, plus effective presentation of analysis results.

title: Health Care Organization and Delivery
description: This course provides an understanding of our complex health system, from basic organization and management to an exploration of the relationships between cost, quality, and access in the context of system improvement and healthcare reform.

title: Exploring Management Topics in the Nonprofit Sector
description: This course will provide an introduction to the key principles and practical challenges in the law, management and marketing of nonprofit organizations (NPOs) and non-governmental organizations (NGOs).

title: Business Process Simulation
description: This course will establish basic proficiency in simulation model development, verification, validation, and analysis.

title: Research Paper
description: Development of a thesis-type paper on a topic of interest to student.

title: Directed Project
description: Permits a student to pursue a defined problem in an intensive way under the direction of a graduate faculty member.

title: Independent Study
description: An in-depth study of a topic of interest to the student and faculty member.

title: Graduation Only
description: • Limited to MBA students. All requirements of the MBA program in progress or completed.

title: Thesis
description: Development and completion of research project using formal investigative methodology.

title: Materials Science Academic Skills
description: Development of academic skills including reading, writing, presenting, and teamwork that are essential for academic success in Materials Science or Materials Science and Engineering.

title: Special Topics in Materials Science
description: Lecture and possibly laboratory or field work in the study of current topics in Materials Science of special interest to students and faculty.

title: Materials Science Junior Seminar
description: Career preparation for Materials Science majors, including ethics and other discussions relevant to the major. Will include seminars with external speakers and class discussions of relevant issues.

title: Directed Studies
description: This course is designed to allow a single student or a group of students to pursue their educational goals and interests under the direction of a faculty member.

title: Independent Study - Juniors
description: This course provides junior-level students with research opportunities in Materials Science or Materials Science & Engineering.

title: Materials Science Capstone I
description: First of a two-course capstone sequence. This course emphasizes hands-on, project-based experiences that are formulated and executed by the student.

title: Materials Science Capstone II
description: Second of a two-semester capstone sequence. This course emphasizes writing projects based on student designed experiments.

title: Special Topics in Advanced Materials Science
description: Lecture and possibly laboratory or field work in the study of current topics of Materials Science of special interest to advanced students and faculty.

title: Independent Study - Seniors
description: This course provides senior-level students with research opportunities in Materials Science or Materials Science & Engineering.

title: Introduction to Engineering
description: A comprehensive study of the engineering design process. Discussion of engineering disciplines with comparisons. The laboratory portion of the course includes design projects from various engineering disciplines.

title: Living in a Materials World
description: The interplay of the structure, properties, and processing of technological materials and their impact on performance. Societal benefits of sustainable, biomimetic, or responsible materials selection.

title: Introduction to Computer Aided Design
description: The course provides an introduction to 3-dimensional computer aided design and modeling.

title: Engineering Sophomore Seminar
description: Career preparation for materials science, materials science and engineering, and biomedical engineering majors. Will include seminars with external speakers and class discussions of relevant issues.

title: Research Experience - MSE
description: This course provides research opportunities in Materials Science & Engineering for students in their first or second year of their major studies.

title: Student Academic Apprenticeship
description: Student academic apprentices in materials science and engineering will assist the course instructor, will aid in the learning of students enrolled in the connected course, and will demonstrate learning about the instruction process and a deeper understanding of materials science and engineering.

title: Engineering Statistics
description: Principles of engineering experimentation and data collection, elementary probability distributions, use of confidence intervals and significance tests in engineering design and decision-making, use of statistical software, design of experiments, statistical process control

title: Materials Characterization
description: A survey of commonly used materials characterization methods (XPS, SEM, AFM, XRD, XRF), including their theory of operation and hands-on experience. Includes a discussion of the measurement process and instrumental analysis of samples.

title: Soft Materials
description: Includes in-depth soft materials topics such as synthesis and processing, structure-property relationships, and applications of soft materials.

title: Thermodynamics of Materials
description: Survey of the laws of thermodynamics and their application in Materials Science including phase equilibria. Mathematical skills relevant to engineering applications are discussed in the lab section.

title: Phase Transformation & Kinetics
description: Phase transformations are explored with emphasis on microstructure development, the impact of diffusion, and nucleation/growth mechanisms.

title: Microelectronic Materials Processing
description: The fabrication of microelectronic devices is discussed, and the thermodynamics and kinetics that impact process design. Methods and practices are reviewed.

title: Macroprocessing of Materials
description: Processing approaches for major categories of bulk materials. Topics range from raw materials to forming and finishing of final products.

title: Macroprocessing Materials Lab
description: Practical and analytical aspects of processing techniques and investigation of structure-property-processing relationships using hands-on experiences.

title: Transport Phenomena
description: Principles of momentum, heat, and mass transport. Applications of appropriate differential equations and boundary conditions to solve problems in materials processing.

title: Electrical, Optical and Magnetic Properties of Materials
description: A description of the behaviors of crystalline solids. Topics include crystallography, diffraction, and the electrical, optical and magnetic properties of materials. Semiconducting materials and devices will also be discussed.

title: Engineering Junior Seminar
description: Career preparation for materials science and engineering and biomedical engineering majors, including ethics and other discussions relevant to the major. Will include seminars with external speakers and class discussions of topical issues.

title: Directed Studies
description: This course is designed to allow a single student or a group of students to pursue their educational goals and interests under the direction of a faculty member.

title: Failure Analysis and Prevention
description: Analysis of mechanical failure in materials systems, and strategies for mitigating failure risks.

title: Computational Materials Science
description: Theory and application of computational methods to model, understand and predict the behavior of materials. Labs provide hands-on experience in solving real materials problems using computational approaches.

title: Nanomaterials
description: Quantum behavior and statistical mechanics of nanomaterials, plus modern synthesis methods, electronic and optical applications, biomaterials.

title: MS&E Capstone I
description: First of a two-course capstone sequence. Hands-on, project-based experiences including engineering design, problem solving and Computer Aided Design (CAD).

title: MS&E Capstone II
description: Second of a two-course capstone sequence. Conclusion of student-designed projects accompanied by preparation of multiple technical documents.

title: Collaborative Internship
description: This course provides students an opportunity to participate in collaborative projects that are performed on campus under the supervision of a faculty member.

title: Off-campus Materials Science Internship
description: Off-campus internship with a regional company or other partner, including professional development training and reflection.

title: Basic Mathematics and Elementary Algebra
description: Develops basic mathematics and elementary algebraic skills necessary for further study of algebra.

title: Intermediate Algebra
description: The real number system, linear equations and inequalities, operations on polynomials and rational expressions, factoring, solutions of quadratic equations, Cartesian coordinates, functions, and related applications.

title: Supplemental Statistics Preparation
description: Development of foundational skills and concepts, and practice with key MATH 246 topics, to allow concurrent enrollment in MATH 246 in lieu of prerequisite enrollment in MATH 20 .

title: Elementary Statistics with Algebra Review I
description: Basic statistical analysis, including descriptive statistics, probability, and sampling distributions, along with review of key algebra skills to support statistical understanding.

title: Supplemental Math Preparation
description: Development of foundational skills and concepts to allow concurrent enrollment in certain credit-bearing mathematics courses in lieu of prerequisite enrollment in MATH 20 .

title: Finite Mathematics with Applications
description: This course will cover applications from Economics, Social and Biological Sciences. The topics include sets, functions, counting principles, probability, solutions of linear systems by graphical and algebraic methods, matrices, linear programming, problem formulation, and using graphs and trees.

title: Introduction to Mathematical Thinking
description: An accessible introduction to some interesting mathematical topics that require analytical thinking and abstract reasoning skills.

title: Mathematics in Latin American Cultures
description: This course introduces important mathematical concepts and topics, such as number and arithmetic systems, symmetry, and data structure, using the cultural lenses of pre-Columbian Latin American indigenous cultures.

title: Algebra for Calculus
description: Algebraic concepts, techniques, and applications including polynomial and rational expressions, linear and quadratic equations, complex numbers, inequalities, absolute value, functions and graphs, exponential and logarithmic functions, systems of equations and inequalities, and zeros of polynomials. This course is for students pursuing degree programs that require calculus.

title: A Short Course in Calculus
description: Primarily for students in biology, business, economics, psychology, and sociology. Functions, limits, continuity, derivatives, integrals, and applications.

title: Precalculus Mathematics
description: Prepares students to enter the Math 114, Math 215, Math 216 sequence. Includes absolute value; logarithmic, exponential, and trigonometric functions; inequalities; conic sections; complex numbers; and topics from theory of equations.

title: Trigonometry
description: Graphs, properties, and geometric significance of trigonometric functions of a real variable. Other topics include trigonometric equations and identities, inverse trigonometric functions and applications.

title: Calculus I
description: Limits, theory, and application of the derivative; introduction to integration.

title: Introduction to the Actuarial Career
description: Designed to start or further efforts in understanding the actuarial profession and to prepare students for an actuarial job search.

title: Introduction to Math Teaching
description: This course seeks to extend student understanding of the mathematics teaching profession and address introductory topics and skills relevant to the teaching and learning of mathematics.

title: Number and Operations I
description: Mathematical knowledge for teachers based on practice/content standards. Topics include: number systems, number theory, and integer algorithms.

title: Number and Operations II
description: Mathematical knowledge for teachers based on practice/content standards. Topics include: fractions, ratios/proportions, and decimals.

title: Calculus II
description: Applications and techniques of integration; improper integrals; sequences and series; power series and Taylor's formula.

title: Calculus III
description: Introduction to functions of several variables, including partial derivatives, multiple integrals, the calculus of vector-valued functions, and Green's Theorem, Stokes' Theorem, and the Divergence Theorem.

title: Elementary Statistics
description: Basic statistical analysis, including descriptive statistics, probability, confidence intervals, hypothesis testing, simple linear regression, correlation, Chi-Square, and Analysis of Variance.

title: Elementary Statistics with Algebra Review II
description: Basic statistical analysis, including confidence intervals and hypothesis tests for one and two populations, chi-squared tests, one-way ANOVA, and linear regression, along with review of key algebra skills to support statistical understanding.

title: Actuarial Communications
description: This course will feature three actuarial case studies from different actuarial disciplines. Employer partners will provide instruction and guidance for students. Focus will be on small group project management and presentations. Students will learn strategies for various business communications and will gain exposure to important real-life actuarial applications.

title: Special Topics
description: Topics will vary, depending on interest of students and faculty.

title: Developmental Tutoring Techniques
description: Training and tutoring for developmental mathematics courses.

title: Algebraic Thinking
description: Mathematical knowledge for teachers based on practice/content standards. Topics include: algebraic equations, linear functions, non-linear functions, and introduction to formal justification.

title: Probability and Statistical Thinking
description: Mathematical knowledge for teachers based on practice/content standards. Topics include: statistics, probability, and expected value.

title: Geometric Thinking
description: Mathematical knowledge for teachers based on practice/content standards. Topics include: 2D & 3D figures, and transformations.

title: Mathematics and Music
description: Using mathematics to understand, appreciate, and create music. Free audio processing software will be emphasized. Rock, jazz, classical, and experimental music will be analyzed. Connections are made to brain science and medicine.

title: Differential Equations
description: Ordinary differential equations of first and second order, linear differential equations, Laplace transforms, series solutions, and systems of linear differential equations.

title: Differential Equations and Linear Algebra
description: Linear algebra: basis, dimension, matrix algebra, determinants, inverses, systems of linear equations, eigenvalues/eigenvectors. (Optional) matrices as linear transformations. Differential equations: first-order linear, separable; second-order linear with constant coefficients; higher order differential equations; first-order linear systems with constant coefficients; Laplace transforms; power series solutions. (Optional) Proof of Existence and Uniqueness Theorems.

title: Digital Signal Processing
description: Fundamentals of digital signal processing. Fourier series, Fourier transforms and computerized Fourier transforms (FFTs) are described and applied to the analysis of digitized audio and digitized images.

title: Discrete Mathematics
description: Introductory course in discrete mathematics. Topics include set theory, logic, sequences, recursion, relations, functions, graph theory, and combinatorics. Also covers methods of proof.

title: Introduction to Real Analysis
description: Real numbers, introductory topological topics, limits, sequences and series of numbers, continuity, and derivatives.

title: Introduction to Real Analysis II
description: The Riemann-Stieltjes Integral, sequences and series of functions, metric spaces, differentiation in high-dimensional Euclidean space, the inverse and implicit function theorems, and further topics in analysis selected by the instructor such as measure, functions of bounded variation, and existence and uniqueness theorems in differential equations.

title: Introduction to Complex Variables
description: Elementary functions of a complex variable, complex integrals, and residue theory.

title: Abstract Algebra for Elementary Teachers
description: Includes sets, functions, and abstract algebraic structures such as groups, rings, and fields.

title: Linear Algebra
description: An introductory course in linear algebra including matrix algebra, systems of linear equations, vector spaces, linear transformations, eigenvalues, and applications. Also covers methods of proof and a survey of student-faculty research.

title: Modern Geometry
description: Contemporary and classical Euclidean geometry, with emphasis on constructions, transformations, and proofs. The Mira and geometry software will be used to illustrate these topics. Some non-Euclidean geometry included.

title: Introduction to Point-Set Topology
description: Theory of sets, continuity of functions, neighborhoods of points, topological and metric spaces, connectedness and compactness.

title: Introduction to Differential Geometry
description: An introduction to the study of curves and surfaces. Topics on curves include curvature, torsion, and the Frenet frames. Topics on surfaces include curvature, minimal surfaces, and Gauss' Remarkable Theorem.

title: Relativity and Differential Geometry
description: The theories and history of special and general relativity. Manifolds, tensors, and metrics as they relate to relativity. Modern research in relativity and astronomy.

title: Classical Number Theory
description: Classical number theory including divisibility, primes and their properties, theory of congruences, Diophantine equations, number theoretic functions, primitive roots, and selected optional topics.

title: Introduction to Probability and Mathematical Statistics
description: Counting techniques, discrete and continuous random variables, probability distributions, sampling distributions, estimation, hypothesis testing, linear regression, correlation, nonparametric statistics. Students who desire more extensive probability and statistics should take Math 346, Math 347.

title: Introduction to Probability
description: Probability in discrete and continuous sample spaces; conditional probability; counting techniques; probability functions; binomial, Poisson, normal distributions; and transformations of variables.

title: Mathematical Statistics
description: Sampling distributions; estimation and properties; hypothesis testing; analysis of variance; regression; nonparametric tests; and introduction to Bayesian inference.

title: Introduction to Financial Mathematics
description: Mathematical theory of interest, annuities and applications, loans, portfolio management, securities valuation, and an introduction to financial derivatives.

title: Numerical Analysis I
description: Nonlinear equations, interpolation and approximation, least squares, systems of linear equations, and error analysis.

title: Introduction to Mathematical Modeling
description: Introduction to building mathematical models in an applied context, including principles of modeling; project(s) involve modeling open-ended real-world problems. Skills covered may include discrete dynamical systems, differential equations, stochastic models, and linear programming.

title: Patterns of Problem Solving
description: Provides tools and concepts needed in problem solving in a variety of disciplines.

title: Issues in Secondary Mathematics
description: An integrated study of mathematical topics taught in grades 5-12 with a focus on increasing pedagogical content knowledge. Topics covered include algebraic, geometric, and other relevant issues in secondary mathematics.

title: Research Methods
description: Introduction to research methods in mathematics. Topics include: background research, methods of proof, mathematical writing, and presentation skills.

title: Directed Studies
description: For the student who wishes to pursue directed study of a topic in mathematics under the supervision of a member of the mathematics department.

title: Academic Apprenticeship
description: Supervised experience with teaching techniques used in a UWEC mathematics course. May involve work in the classroom and/or outside of class.

title: Independent Study - Juniors
description: Individual project under the direction of a faculty member.

title: Abstract Algebra I
description: An introduction to the abstract concepts of algebra such as groups, rings, and fields. Topics such as direct products, group homomorphisms, factor groups, integral domains, and polynomial rings will be included.

title: Abstract Algebra II
description: A continuation of Math 425 including more advanced topics from groups, rings, and fields, such as the Sylow theorems, prime and maximal ideals, unique factorization domains, and finite fields.

title: Digital Image Processing
description: Applications of Fourier analysis and wavelets to optics and image processing. Topics include: diffraction, wave optical theory of lenses and imaging, wavelets, and image processing.

title: Linear Regression Analysis, with Time Series
description: Simple and multiple regression, transformation of variables, model checking, analysis of residuals, regression diagnostics, stepwise regression, index variables, confidence intervals, and computer software; overview of time series and forecasting methods.

title: Advanced Statistical Modeling
description: Teaches students the key concepts of generalized linear models, principal components analysis, decision tree models, and cluster analysis. Students will learn the purpose and uses of these methods, make interpretation of obtained results, and gain knowledge of their various applications. Applications will be implemented using the statistical software R / Rstudio.

title: Experimental Design and Analysis
description: Basic concepts of statistical design of experiments; linear models; randomized block, Latin square, factorial, fractional factorial, rotatable, and incomplete block designs and applications.

title: Survey Sampling
description: Survey sampling including random sampling, stratified sampling, cluster sampling, and design of surveys.

title: Nonparametric Statistics
description: Testing and estimation based on the binomial distribution, contingency tables, Wilcoxon Tests, rank correlation, Kruskal-Wallis and Friedman analysis of variance. Comparisons with parametric methods.

title: Foundations of Actuarial Science
description: Actuarial theory of financial mathematical models for contingent events as applied to insurance, annuities, pensions, and reserving. Together with Math 460, this course prepares students for the actuarial exam covering contingent payments.

title: Teaching Mathematics with Technology
description: In this course students will investigate teaching mathematics effectively with technology. Technology used includes graphing calculators, spreadsheets, interactive software, and calculator based laboratories. Topics include functions, statistics, probability, geometry, and mathematical modeling.

title: Contingent Payment Analysis
description: Actuarial theory of reserves, multiple life models, multiple decrement models, Poisson processes, and multi-state transition models. Together with Math 450, this course prepares students for the actuarial exam covering contingent payments.

title: History of Mathematics
description: A study of mathematics from a historical and worldwide point of view beginning with ancient mathematics and including selected mathematicians and periods of mathematical development up to the present.

title: Mathematical Models for Financial Economics
description: An introduction to financial derivatives including forwards and options with coverage of binomial, Black-Scholes, and lognormal pricing models.

title: Actuarial Foundations of Short-Term Coverages
description: An introduction to ratemaking and reserving for short-term insurance coverages; credibility theory; and actuarial models for frequency, severity, and aggregate distributions.

title: Research Seminar
description: Individual research projects in mathematics under the supervision of a member of the mathematics faculty, culminating in research project presentations.

title: Special Topics
description: A variable content course designed to allow a breadth of study through investigation of mathematical topics not covered in other courses. Special interests of instructors will be utilized to provide topics.

title: Capstone Seminar
description: An intensive study of selected topics including written and/or oral presentations by the students. The topics will connect ideas from across the mathematics major.

title: Mathematics Seminar
description: An intensive study of selected topics in mathematics. The exact topics to be studied will vary according to the interests of the professor and the seminar participants.

title: Honors Seminar
description: An intensive study of selected topics in mathematics. The exact topics to be studied will vary according to the interests of the professor and the seminar participants.

title: Mathematics Internship
description: Provides supervised on-the-job experience in an area of interest with mathematical content.

title: Independent Study - Seniors
description: Individual project under the direction of a faculty member.

title: Special Topics
description: A variable content course designed to allow a breadth of study through investigation of mathematical topics not covered in other courses. Special interests of instructors will be utilized to provide topics.

title: Mathematics Seminar
description: An intensive study of selected topics in mathematics. The exact topics to be studied will vary according to the interests of the professor and the seminar participants.

title: Independent Study
description: Individual project under the direction of a faculty member.

title: Leadership and Personal Development
description: Introduction to the Army profession and military leadership. Personal development includes goal setting, time management, physical fitness and stress management. Lab includes squad movement, map reading, first aid and formations. Physical Training is composed of techniques to improve muscular strength and endurance and cardio-respiratory capacity. Includes strength and aerobic exercises, water confidence training, distance running, military drills and team sports. Physical Training attendance required one day per week. Open to all students.

title: Foundations of Leadership
description: Basic leadership fundamentals such as problem solving, oral and written communication, goal setting and tactical knowledge. Exploration of leadership theories, attributes and traits. Lab includes team tactics, map reading, marksmanship and marching techniques. Physical Training consists of techniques to improve muscular strength and endurance and cardio-respiratory capacity. Includes strength and aerobic exercises, water confidence training, distance running, military drills and team sports. Physical Training attendance required one day per week. Open to all students.

title: Leadership and Ethics
description: Explores innovative tactical leadership strategies and styles by examining team dynamics, leadership theories and principles emphasizing leadership analysis and leadership self-assessment. Examination of Army Values and Ethics and their relationship to the Law of Armed Conflict and the philosophy of military service. Students broaden their knowledge of communications, land navigation and tactics, which are practiced in lab. Physical Training attendance required one day per week. Open to all students.

title: Army Doctrine and Decision-Making
description: Students gain an insight to analytical techniques, creative thinking skills and the Army problem solving process. Develop an understanding of troop leading procedures, operations orders, and squad and platoon tactics. Basic concepts of offensive, defensive, multidomain, and joint operations. Lab applies basic team leadership during practical exercises in a field environment. Physical Training consists of techniques to improve muscular strength and endurance and cardio-respiratory capacity. Includes strength and aerobic exercises, water confidence training, distance running, military drills and team sports. Physical Training attendance required one day per week. Open to all students.

title: Training Management and the Warfighting Functions
description: Students conduct leadership self-assessment, receiving direct and peer feedback. Apply advanced tactical skills and leadership in planning small-unit tactical training applying problem-solving techniques. Apply the fundamentals of Training Management and how the Army operates through the Warfighting Functions. Lab reinforces squad-level tactics, employing the troop-leading procedures. Physical Training consists of developing and leading programs to improve muscular strength and endurance and cardio-respiratory capacity. Includes strength and aerobic exercises, water confidence training, distance running, military drills and team sports. Physical Training attendance required three days per week. Open to all students.

title: Applied Team Leadership
description: Emphasis on writing operation orders, improving oral/written communications, and leadership analysis. Receive direct and peer feedback on leadership performance. Lab applies advanced tactics and leadership during patrol-size tactical training. Physical Training consists of developing and leading programs to improve muscular strength and endurance and cardio-respiratory capacity. Includes strength and aerobic exercises, water confidence training, distance running, military drills and team sports. Physical Training attendance required three days per week.

title: US Military Experience from 1607 to the Present
description: The US Army Cadet Command Military Experience course covers military history from early colonial warfare in the seventeenth century to the present. The purpose of this course is to lead students in understanding the role military officers have played in the development of our country.

title: Military Science and Leadership Independent Study
description: Students propose an independent study project on a topic in military history, military tactics or political/military leadership to the Assistant Professor of Military Science. Projects may include library research or original empirical research. Students work independently, with supervising instructor providing oversight and assistance. Physical Training attendance required three days a week. Open to juniors and seniors.

title: Developing Adaptive Leaders
description: Develop proficiency in planning and executing complex operations, functioning as a staff member and mentoring subordinates. Explore training management, staff collaboration and developmental counseling techniques. Lab instruction is designed to increase students’ organizational-level, mentoring and assessment skills. Physical Training requires mentoring junior Cadets in techniques to improve muscular strength and endurance and cardio-respiratory capacity. Includes strength and aerobic exercises, water confidence training, distance running, military drills and team sports. Physical Training attendance required three days per week.

title: Leadership in a Complex World
description: Examine officership, study Army programs, and complete a battle analysis. Examine civilian, governmental, legal, and cultural factors of conducting military operations in an international environment. Lab instruction is designed to increase students’ organizational level, mentoring and assessment skills. Physical Training requires mentoring junior Cadets in techniques to improve muscular strength and endurance and cardio-respiratory capacity. Includes strength and aerobic exercises, water confidence training, distance running, military drills and team sports. Physical Training attendance required three days per week. Open to all students.

title: Performing Arts On Stage
description: Introduction to music listening and theatre appreciation. The course provides a working vocabulary for describing music and theatre while exploring the connection between performing arts and cultural context. In-class lectures/demonstrations are presented by performing arts faculty.

title: Fundamentals of Music
description: Introduction to musical skills and notation: reading and notating simple music; singing; playing melodies, chords, and scales on recorder and piano; basics of pitch, rhythm, timbre, form, and texture.

title: Introduction to Electric Guitar
description: Designed for students with little or no prior experience playing the electric guitar. Students will be introduced to the fundamentals of guitar technique and its repertoire through exercises, songs, and solo excerpts. This course aims to provide basic concepts of improvisation, harmonization, comping techniques and the basic technical skills and musical vocabulary necessary to play solos and improvise on the electric guitar.

title: Introduction to Piano
description: An introduction to the keyboard for the non-music major/minor. Music reading, harmonization, improvisation, and basic music theory concepts will be emphasized. For the student with no prior keyboard knowledge.

title: Class Piano
description: Fundamental piano knowledge with an introduction to repertoire, technique, harmonization, and sight-reading.

title: Advanced Class Piano
description: Designed for the student with some knowledge of piano. Sightreading, harmonization, transposition, technique, and repertoire are stressed in preparation for the proficiency examination given at course conclusion.

title: Music Appreciation
description: Master works and historical eras of Western music; nontechnical, offered for enjoyment and enrichment of cultural background.

title: Global Roots of Music in the United States
description: Forms and cultures represented in folk music in the United States with study of global roots of music and examination of perspectives and roles of music in culture.

title: History of Rock and Pop
description: Non-technical stylistic development and history of popular music in the United States and United Kingdom from approximately 1955 through 1985. Emphasis placed on developing perceptive listening skills and analyzing cultural and social contexts.

title: Evolution of Jazz
description: Non-technical history and appreciation of jazz.

title: Folk Guitar in Culture across Time
description: Beginning folk guitar course with diversity infused to represent diverse repertoire and roles and styles of guitar playing in the United States and global music scene.

title: Beginning Guitar
description: Designed for students with little or no prior experience playing guitar. Students will learn the fundamentals of guitar technique and its repertoire through exercises and popular songs. Additionally, students will be introduced to music theory and music notation applied to the guitar and acquire a basic historical understanding of the guitar’s role in different cultures.

title: Introduction to Singing
description: Rudiments of singing for the general student, including breath management, tone production, and experience with classical and popular singing styles.

title: The Healing Art of Music
description: Students will learn how music is processed neurologically so as to be able to intentionally use music to elicit desired responses. Students will learn to structure musical engagement to elicit healthy responses and eliminate or decrease unhealthy responses for oneself and for others via weekly interaction with a community partner. Students will learn how to interact with and motivate others to respond in a healthy manner and achieve desired goals through musical engagement.

title: Music Theory Fundamentals
description: This course covers fundamental concepts of written music theory and aural skills for musical theatre majors, music minors, and Recording Arts Certificate students.

title: Theory Written I
description: Triads and their inversions, principles of voice leading, and first through fourth species counterpoint.

title: Theory Aural I
description: Skills in aural recognition and sight-singing, including simple melodies and basic techniques related to melodic and harmonic dictation.

title: Theory Written II
description: Continuation of Theory Written I, with addition of phrase structure, seventh chords, non-harmonic tones, secondary chords, common-chord modulation, fugue, and fifth-species counterpoint.

title: Theory Aural II
description: Continuation of Theory Aural I, with addition of seventh chords, non-harmonic tones, secondary dominants, and modulation to closely related keys.

title: Beginning Composition
description: Introduction to principles of music composition with an emphasis on recent art music. Composition and performance of student works for one to five players.

description: Solo repertoire, techniques, and performance.

title: String Techniques
description: Materials, methods and techniques of playing and teaching violin, viola, cello, and bass through lecture and lab performance.

title: Percussion Techniques
description: Materials, methods, and techniques of playing and teaching percussion instruments through lecture and lab performance.

title: Brass Techniques
description: Materials, methods, and techniques of playing and teaching trumpet, horn, trombone, euphonium, and tuba through lecture and lab performance.

title: Vocal Techniques
description: Materials, methods, and techniques of teaching voice and choral ensembles.

title: Woodwind Techniques
description: Materials, methods, and techniques of playing and teaching flute, clarinet, and saxophone through lecture and lab performance.

title: Woodwind Techniques
description: Materials, methods, and techniques of playing and teaching oboe and bassoon through lecture and lab performance.

title: Topics in Sound Recording
description: Lecture/Discussion Hours: VariableTopics will vary depending on the interests of students and faculty. Topics will address various concepts and techniques in sound recording techniques and technology.

title: Music and Gender
description: Addresses the relationship between music and gender, how music reflects and influences gender construction, and the role of women in musical culture (composers, song-writers, performers, and patrons). No prior knowledge of music assumed.

title: Global Traditions in Music
description: Introduction to the sound and concepts of diverse musical cultures from around the world, examining broad historical, cultural, and social contexts of music. Draws on folk, popular, traditional and art music genres. No prior knowledge of music assumed.

title: Music History & Society I
description: Development of European classical music to 1700 with comparative music examples from folk, popular, and jazz traditions.

title: Music History & Society II
description: Development of Euro-American classical music from 1700 with comparative music examples from folk, popular, and jazz music traditions.

title: Dance and Relaxation Neuromedical Applications
description: Students will learn Alexander Technique and other physical – therapy based relaxation techniques to facilitate relaxation and healthy movement using music. Students will learn Neurologic Music Therapy Patterned Sensory Enhancement techniques to help long-term care residents to perform their range of motion exercises. Students will learn and apply relaxation techniques paired with music for local youth.

title: Theory Written III
description: Continuation of Theory Written II, with the addition of Neapolitan, augmented sixth, and chromatic mediant chords, modal mixture, advanced modulation, and binary, ternary, and sonata form.

title: Theory Aural III
description: Continuation of Theory Aural II, with the addition of altered chords, chords of multiple thirds, Neapolitan sixth chord, augmented sixth chords, and advanced modulation.

title: Theory Written IV
description: Continuation of Theory Written III, with the addition of late nineteenth-century chromaticism and form, and techniques of twentieth-century composers.

title: Theory Aural IV
description: Continuation of Theory Aural III, with the addition of material employed by composers of the mid-late nineteenth century and the twentieth century.

title: University Chamber Orchestra
description: Lab/Studio Hours: 2

title: String Ensemble
description: String chamber music which may result in a public performance.

title: Brass Ensemble
description: Brass chamber music which may result in a public performance.

title: Woodwind Ensemble
description: Woodwind chamber music which may result in a public performance.

title: Contemporary Music Ensemble
description: Includes study and performances of contemporary compositions as well as 20th- and 21st-century repertoire and styles.

title: Keyboard Accompanying
description: Focuses on development of accompanying/collaborative skills of pianists. Vocal and instrumental repertoire studied with a focus on efficient preparation, effective rehearsing, and sensitive and artistic collaboration.

title: Functional Piano
description: Beginning melodic realization by ear, simple accompaniment patterns, harmonization using primary chords, transposition and modulation, song arrangements, improvisation, and lead-sheet realization.

title: Opera/Music Theatre Workshop
description: Development of performance abilities and technical skills necessary for production of opera and music theatre culminating in performance of operatic and/or music theatre repertoire.

title: Music and Culture Abroad
description: Emphasis on music and culture of the host country.

title: Special Topics
description: Topics will vary according to interests of students and faculty. Focus is on aspects of music and music education not extensively covered in regular courses.

title: Sophomore Recital
description: Preparation and performance of public recital.

title: Analyzing Global Traditions in Music
description: Addresses musical cultures from around the world through broad cultural and specific technical perspectives. Comparisons are made to Western musical practices.

title: Music in the United States
description: This course will examine American music from the Colonial Period to the present and particularly highlight music within the contexts of class and race. Significant cultural, social, and historical relationships to the musical developments will be explored.

title: Electronic Music
description: Discussion, exposure, and experimentation in synthesizer techniques (ARP 2600); patching between studio modules; tape composition.

title: Eurhythmics
description: Elements of music and musical expression through the medium of bodily movement.

title: Music in Early Childhood
description: Theories of early childhood development, strategies for structuring musical experiences, and uses of music to facilitate growth in other areas.

title: Piano Pedagogy
description: Survey problems of individual and class instruction, learning methods, in-depth study of piano method books and literature, the physiological basis of piano playing, sound business practices for the studio teacher, and a brief survey of historical schools of piano instruction.

title: Sound Technology Principles
description: This course focuses on principles of live sound mixing and recording, including hands-on work in recording studios.

title: Orchestration
description: Range, transposition, playing characteristics, and practical use of the instruments.

title: Songwriting Seminar
description: This is a project-based course in which students practice the art of songwriting and explore how lyrics, melody, harmony, and structure make a great song. Each student will write or co-write multiple songs.

title: Sound Technology Principles II
description: This course supplements the knowledge of Sound Technology Principles, introducing students to a new set of skills to polish their recordings and productions. Included are sound technology principles such as: intermediate and advanced editing techniques, mastering, and synthesis. In addition, students will build on existing mixing skills and learn about different techniques and technologies with EQ and dynamics processing.

title: Influence of Music on Behavior
description: Students will learn neuroscience models related to how music is processed in the brain as well as how musical interventions are structured to address goals in motor, cognitive, language, social, and emotional functioning. Students will apply these principles for themselves and for people in the community with special needs by creating musical interventions to target each goal area.

title: Counterpoint
description: This course covers instrumental counterpoint in two and three voices based on eighteenth-century techniques, and it includes a summary of the legacy of sixteenth-century vocal techniques.

title: Jazz History and Analysis
description: Technical survey of jazz history with music analysis.

title: Jazz Arranging
description: Arranging for small and large ensembles in the jazz idiom.

title: Jazz Improvisation
description: An introductory course in the art of jazz improvisation with emphasis on performance by the student.

title: Voice Pedagogy
description: Development of skills for teaching singing through the study of the vocal mechanism, processes of respiration, resonance, and articulation. Application of these skills in lesson work with college, high school and middle school singers.

title: Singers' Diction: Italian and German
description: Study of Italian and German lyric diction. Designed primarily for singers.

title: Singers' Diction: English and French
description: Study of English and French lyric diction. Designed primarily for singers.

title: Conducting
description: A basic course in fundamental, practical skills of baton and rehearsal techniques for both instrumental and choral music.

description: This seminar focuses on the systematic method of score study with a purpose of developing own musical interpretation. Students will select and prepare an interpretation project and present results to class.

title: Music History Seminar
description: Topics vary depending on interests of students and faculty and may be specific themes, styles, composers, or eras in history. Involves readings on the selected topic and preparation of a research project by each student.

title: Music Theory Seminar
description: Topics will vary depending on the interests of students and faculty. Students will develop a focused research project based on their interests, present preliminary results to the class, and write a final paper.

title: Marching Band Procedures
description: A study of methods and techniques used in the administration and instruction of contemporary marching band. The course presents a specific system of conceiving, writing, and teaching marching band shows; and explores current technology.

title: Voice Ensemble
description: Preparation, interpretation and performance of duets, trios, quartets, and other repertoire composed for one solo voice per part; geared toward Bachelor of Music-Voice students.

title: Opera Literature
description: The historical and stylistic developments in opera from its beginnings to the present through examination of the operatic repertoire.

title: Voice Literature
description: The study of solo voice literature, its interpretation and stylistic development from the seventeenth century to the present.

title: Advanced Instrumental Techniques
description: Intensive study of performance techniques and pedagogy of a single instrument or family of instruments; includes the physical, psychological, and aesthetic aspects of performance, methodology and literature, equipment and maintenance.

title: Directing Practicum - Cabaret Productions
description: This course will include instruction in the areas of choral arranging, instrumental arranging, choreography, costuming, and rehearsal techniques in preparation for the annual music variety show.

title: Choral Workshop

title: Jazz Arranging Workshop
description: Instruction in techniques of arranging and scoring in jazz tradition and styles.

title: Percussion Workshop

title: Directed Studies-Literature (Major Instrument)

description: Intensive study of performance techniques and pedagogy of a single instrument or family of instruments; includes the physical, psychological, and aesthetic aspects of performance, methodology and literature, equipment and maintenance.

title: Elementary Music Education Workshop
description: For elementary classroom teachers and music specialists. Designed to meet individual needs of workshop participants. Lectures and demonstrations of classroom materials, procedures, techniques, and publications.

title: Special Topics
description: Topics will vary according to interests of students and faculty. Focus is on aspects of music and music education not extensively covered in regular courses.

title: Choral Workshop
description: Develops an understanding of vocal and conducting problems through the study, rehearsal, and performance of choral literature representative of specific historical periods. Specific topics to be announced and published annually.

title: Jazz Arranging Workshop
description: Instruction in techniques of arranging and scoring in jazz tradition and styles.

title: Percussion Workshop
description: For instrumental music directors who need to improve their knowledge of percussion. Review and/or introduction of basic pedagogy and practical care of instruments.

title: Independent Study
description: Individual project under the direction of a faculty member.

title: Introduction to Music Education
description: Introduces basic issues and principles of music teaching with an emphasis on observation in classrooms, reflective thinking, comprehensive musicianship, teaching standards, and music content standards.

title: Adaptive Music
description: Structuring activities, adapting environments for students with special needs (exceptionalities), using techniques, adaptive instruments, academic and technology resources. Lab day with focus on guitar applications and improvisational structures. Field experiences include observation and teacher assisting for approximately 10 hours.

title: Principles, Practices, and Practicum in General Music PreK-5
description: Pedagogical focus on musical concepts, current and emerging instructional models, methods, materials, and strategies for designing and assessing developmentally appropriate learning experiences for pre-K--5 music classes. Includes observation and teaching in public schools for approximately 10 - 12 hours with emphasis upon teacher standards and reflective practice.

title: Music as Content in Schools
description: Addresses philosophy of music education, curriculum development, lesson and unit planning, assessment, and management of music classrooms. Includes field experience in which student will experience teaching under the discretion of a cooperating teacher.

title: Choral Literature and Conducting
description: Explores choral literature, performance practice, and conducting techniques. Emphasis is placed on artistic interpretation of traditional, contemporary, and ethnic choral music and the communication of interpretation through gesture.

title: Instrumental Literature and Conducting
description: Explores instrumental literature, performance practice, and conducting techniques. Emphasis is placed on artistic interpretation of traditional and contemporary instrumental music and the communication of interpretation through gesture.

title: Choral Teaching Methods
description: Focuses on instructional models, methods, materials, and strategies for teaching and assessing musical concepts in school choral performance ensembles and classes. Emphasizes curricular development and classroom management based upon stages of development in students.

title: Secondary Instrumental Teaching Methods
description: Focuses on instructional models, methods, materials, and strategies for teaching and assessing musical concepts in school instrumental performance ensembles and classes. Emphasizes curricular development and classroom management based upon stages of development in students.

title: Student Teaching in Music
description: Professional music teaching experience in the school under the supervision of a cooperating teacher in the school and related University music personnel. Students should follow school calendar where assigned.

title: Internship Teaching in Music
description: Professional music teaching internship in the school under the supervision of a cooperating teacher in the school and related University music personnel. Students should follow school calendar where assigned.

title: Special Topics
description: Topics will vary according to interests of students and faculty. Focus is on aspects of music education not extensively covered in regular courses.

title: Independent Study - Seniors
description: An intensive study in music education of particular interest to the student.

title: Special Topics
description: Topics will vary according to interests of students and faculty. Focus is on aspects of music education not extensively covered in regular courses.

title: Cultural Bridge I
description: Provides an opportunity for students from cultures not widely represented at UW-Eau Claire to develop a beginning familiarity with the College of Nursing and the University. Assessment of academic skills and provision for financial aid procedures are included.

title: Cultural Bridge II
description: Continues the preparation of students from cultures not widely represented at UW-Eau Claire to enter the nursing major. Emphasis is on enhancing study skills, library skills, writing skills, and scientific reasoning skills.

title: Cultural Bridge III
description: Continues the preparation of students from cultures not widely represented at UW-Eau Claire to enter the nursing major. Emphasis is on continuing development of library skills and writing skills. Preparation for study of scientific field is included.

title: Dilemmas of Knowledge in Nursing and Healthcare
description: An introductory course on significant problems in nursing and healthcare. Explores ways that knowledge is discovered and used to describe and develop effective solutions.

title: Integrating Knowledge for Improving Health
description: Critical thinking and evidence-based practice approaches used to demonstrate how healthcare professionals analyze, integrate, and apply knowledge to challenges ranging from everyday health care practice to global health policy. Simulations and discussions are used.

title: Wellness: Food for Fitness
description: Introduces nutrition standards and role of nutrients in health. Examines nutrition trends and food industry practices from a consumer's perspective. Emphasizes understanding of personal nutrition and fitness in relation to wellness.

title: Navigating Health Care
description: Emphasis on decision-making in wellness and health care. Includes advocacy for self and others. Consideration of values, financial aspects, effectiveness of choices in promoting wellness. Introduces health care providers and services.

title: Body, Mind, Spirit Wellness
description: Emphasis on an integrated approach to personal wellness, including the body-mind-spirit relationships. Consideration is given to culture, gender, and socioeconomic perspectives and how treatment modalities and health procedures are applied to personal wellness.

title: The Language of Health Care
description: The study of specialized language and terminology used by health care professionals. The study of roots, word elements, word formation, and analyses.

title: Introduction to Public Health
description: Examines population health and disease considering historical and current public health practice. Focus areas include cultural, political, environmental, and socioeconomic influences that increase population vulnerability and risk. Effectiveness of public health interventions is analyzed.

title: Pharmacology for Nursing Practice
description: Introduces concepts of pharmacologic therapy for collaborative nursing practice. Selected medication categories are studied with emphasis on nursing responsibility, accountability, and safety.

title: Normal and Therapeutic Nutrition in Nursing Practice
description: Examines role of human nutrition for population health promotion and as therapy for management of altered physiologic states. Addresses sociopolitical, cultural, spiritual, religious, and food quality and safety aspects of nutrition.

title: Interdisciplinary Studies in Health and Health Care
description: Will vary depending upon the specific course being offered.

title: Foundations of Professional Nursing Practice
description: Explores caring, holistic aspects of nursing along a historical timeline. Concepts include nursing process, communication, advocacy, licensure, scope of practice, ethics, research, theory, quality, safety, and evidence-based practice. Introduces interdisciplinary collaboration and documentation.

title: Women's Health Issues
description: Explores a wide range of women's health issues within the context of developmental changes, environmental influences, normal health processes, wellness, and health concerns. Examines health promotion, cultural diversity, and social relations of gender related to women's health.

title: Practicum: Foundational Skills for Nursing Practice
description: Introduces nursing process, communication, and teamwork in the context of caring and advocacy. Includes skill development in health assessment and interventions including patient teaching, integrative therapies, and medication administration. Campus and clinical laboratory.

title: Scientific Concepts for Nursing
description: Focuses on application of various scientific concepts as they specifically relate to nursing practice. Content arranged to coordinate with body systems and functions.

title: Directed Studies
description: Study of problems or special areas in or related to nursing.

title: Teaching Apprenticeship
description: Qualified students, selected and supervised by faculty, will facilitate teaching and learning in specific course(s). Apprentices enhance their knowledge of nursing and ability to communicate that knowledge to other students.

title: Independent Study
description: Individual project under the direction of a faculty member.

title: Travelers' Health
description: Encompasses pre-travel preparations to post-travel considerations for travel to developing and industrialized countries. Covers commonly encountered illnesses, prevention, and treatments. Empowers learners to be prepared, self-reliant, healthy, and globally-, culturally- and environmentally-aware travelers.

title: Wound Care Associate
description: Introduction to basic principles of skin injury prevention, wound assessment, and wound care. Focuses on collaborative care for patients with acute and chronic wounds. Didactic and clinical skill components.

title: Practicum: Advanced Skills for Nursing Practice
description: Focuses on developing advanced nursing skills within the context of holistic practice. Emphasizes underlying theory. Practicum incorporates clinical reasoning, ethical practice, and implementation of advanced skills with an adult population. Campus and clinical laboratory.

title: Nursing Leadership and Systems I
description: Examines national healthcare systems and models of care, healthcare financing and regulation, collaboration, conflict management, leadership, delegation, and organizational cultures, models and theories.

title: Nursing Care of Adults and Older Adults I
description: Focuses on nursing care of adults/older adults with application of evidence-based practice and nursing process for select acute and chronic conditions.

title: Psychiatric and Mental Health Nursing
description: Introduces basic concepts of psychiatric mental health nursing. Focus is on theory- and evidence-based mental health promotion for at-risk populations and nursing care of people diagnosed with mental illnesses.

title: Nursing Care of Adults and Older Adults II
description: Continued examination of nursing care of adults and older adults with application of evidence-based practice and nursing process for select acute and chronic conditions.

title: Practicum: Nursing Care of Adults and Older Adults
description: Focuses on use of the nursing process to give holistic theory- and evidence-based care to acutely ill adult and older adult patients and their families. Emphasizes therapeutic communication, intraprofessional collaboration, and clinical reasoning. Clinical laboratory.

title: Community and Public Health Nursing
description: Expands on population-focused and public health nursing practice with populations at risk and local and global communities as a whole. Key concepts include epidemiology, health promotion, disease prevention, risk reduction, harm reduction, and evidence-based practice.

title: Pharmacotherapeutics and Pathophysiology for Allied Health Professionals
description: This course provides a basic understanding of disease processes and basic pharmacology for allied health professionals. Common medical and non-medical management will be introduced from a consumer perspective. Collaboration with the client and other health care professionals in optimizing the client's health will be integrated throughout the course.

title: Nursing Care of Women, Infants, and Families
description: Examines evidence-based nursing care with individuals and families experiencing pregnancy, birth, and care of the newborn. Examines human responses from theoretical and cultural perspectives. Health promotion and illness prevention are emphasized.

title: International Dimensions of Health Care
description: Explores health care delivery systems in selected foreign countries as compared to the United States. Examines the cultural, social, political, economic, and environmental factors influencing the development of these systems. May involve international immersion.

title: Men's Health Issues
description: Explores historical, societal, and cultural influences on the prevalent constructions of masculinity and men's health. Examines health promotion, and disease prevention for the male health consumer.

title: Cross Cultural Health
